experts.lens: Data-analysis methodology / statistics: in-sample selection bias, out-of-sample validation, robust estimation, correlated-sample inference, systematic-budget construction, and resolution unfolding
paper1_assessment: [+] Exceptional per-point transparency: Figs 18-26 show every per-bin (DW-UP)/2 and (DW+UP)/2 distribution WITH its Gaussian fit overlaid at all six energies - a referee can audit every sigma that feeds Fig 27. Most test-beam papers hide this; keep it as the house standard.

[+] Honest scope fencing of Eq 1: the paper explicitly states the 52%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31% is shower-max sampling (~3 LYSO tiles) and NOT full-module resolution, pre-empting the obvious misreading. Same honesty in flagging the ~0.2 ns satellite (Fig 19) and its ~15% area rather than silently cutting it.

[-] The headline fit (Eq 2: 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps) is quoted with NO parameter uncertainties, no chi2/ndf, and no a-b correlation - and b=17.5 ps is an extrapolation below the lowest measured point (27 ps at 150 GeV). The 9 amplitude bins are equal-WIDTH (populations collapse in the tails - visible as the noisy outer points in Figs 21-26), and 'BestMinus' selects bins 6-8 post hoc on the same sample it reports: a textbook in-sample argmin. Our own OOS folding shows equal-width best-bin is in-sample optimistic (25.6 -> 27.5 ps OOS), so the published 27.4 happens to be the honest OOS value - but the paper never demonstrates that, it just got lucky.

[-] The six-method comparison (Fig 27 Left) declares BestMinus 'the best estimator' with no statistical test, despite all six methods being evaluated on IDENTICAL events (fully correlated samples - the right comparison is a paired/bootstrap CI on the sigma difference, not eyeballing curves). Related mis-specification: BestPlus-MCP subtracts sigma_MCP ~10 ps in quadrature, but the actual contaminant of (DW+UP)/2 is the ~71-74 ps inter-group DRS4 domino jitter (measured via MCP1-MCP2), not the MCP intrinsic - the quadrature correction targets the wrong term.

[-] No systematic-uncertainty budget anywhere: no fiducial-radius, threshold, fit-range, satellite-inclusion, or run-period variation. Fig 27 Right error bars are statistics-only, so the 17.5 ps floor carries an unquantified and unbounded systematic.

[-] Beam momentum spread is waved at ('understood to be <=2%') but never propagated into Eq 1; at 1% H2-typical it is a non-negligible contributor to the %/E and constant terms of a 9.31% constant-term measurement.
paper2_timing: A locked-pipeline + OOS table as a dedicated Methods subsection: one table listing EVERY optimized choice (timing fiducial r(E)=2.5/3.0 mm, brightest-K=1000, per-build source adoption, quantile best-bin) with columns in-sample value | matched-fraction OOS value | adopted. The matched-FRACTION fold-by-run protocol (not matched-K - the documented half-fold trap) must be stated explicitly. This single table defuses the 'did you tune the cuts?' attack that the published paper is exposed to.

State the source-adoption RULE ex ante, then validate it: 'lgcfd where HG clips (DSB1/MIXED, 71-94% clipped fraction), led where it does not (LuAG/TENERGY)' is a physics decision rule, not an argmin over 8 sources. Print the full 8-source survey per build (the timing_allmethods_* figures) as evidence the rule's winner is robust, and show per-build OOS fold stability of the adopted source. Never present the adopted source as 'the one that fit best'.

Correlated-sample inference for every 'A beats B' claim: cfd05 vs lgcfd on the SAME brightest-1000 events must be quoted as a paired event-bootstrap CI on Delta-sigma(150) (= +3.1 ps) and on Delta-b (22.1 -> 19.5 ps), plus a run-jackknife. The MIXED kill-shot ratio 0.99 (chi2/ndf 0.4/5 - suspiciously low, check for overestimated per-point errors) needs the same paired-bootstrap CI; it is the paper's central claim and currently has no defensible interval.

Sigma-extraction algorithm box: document tebSigma exactly as implemented post-fix (iterative 2.5-sigma truncated RMS debiased by 0.9546 -> robust-windowed 120-bin histogram -> 2-sigma-core Gaussian -> accept only if within 0.5x-2x of robust -> skew/kurtosis tail guard |skew|>3 or kurt>20 falls back to robust) AND the in-event channel-consistency veto (eventDWUP, kTimingChanConsistency_ns). Report the vetoed-event fraction per build per energy (must be small and smooth in E) and sweep the veto threshold as a labeled systematic to prove the veto removes broken-timing outliers without sculpting the Gaussian core.

Monotonicity as a SANITY CHECK, never a constraint: present monotone sigma_t(E) as an emergent property of the fixed estimator; put the audit (30-sigma broken-timing outliers inflating the 5-sigma fit window -> manufactured non-monotonicity; monotonicity_evidence/monotonicity_fix figures) in an appendix as the worked failure-detection example. State explicitly that published cfd05 numbers are unchanged by the fix.

Per-point distribution transparency matching the published standard: per-energy (DW-UP)/2 distributions with fits for the ADOPTED method per build (the method_dist_* set), each panel annotated with N, chi2/ndf, core fraction, and satellite fraction; plus the in-data demonstration that lgcfd removes the 0.2 ns satellite (checkable claim - show the cfd05-vs-lgcfd distribution pair, do not assert).

Floor honesty package: free a/sqrtE + b fits with per-point errors sigma/sqrt(2N), PDG scale factor sqrt(chi2/ndf) when chi2/ndf>1 (DSB1 lgcfd is 2.1 - scaling is mandatory), the a-b correlation (rho ~ -0.88) reported, BOTH 1/sqrtE and 1/E forms shown per build with chi2, per-technique floors kept distinct (cfd05 vs lgcfd are 4.2-sigma apart - never force-share), and the plain statement that LuAG's brightest 150 GeV point (~42 ps) never reaches its extrapolated floor in data. Headline = measured 25.3 ps at 150 GeV; b = confirmation of the published 17.5, never a revision.

Systematics table per build mirroring paperSystematics.C but extended with two missing rows: (i) run-period stability - chronological split of each build's runs (the four builds were taken in different weeks with different beam tunes; cross-build claims inherit this), and (ii) inter-calibration - propagate the <=2% hglg sidecar slope drift and the per-channel a,b fit uncertainty into sigma_t. Keep source choice excluded (it is the thesis) with the exclusion justified in one sentence.

MIXED material-map provenance: the NE/SW=DSB1, NW/SE=LuAG assignment is data-INFERRED from brightness (inferMixed.C) - the kill-shot then argues brightness ordering from those corners. Close the circularity: confirm against the logbook (the flagged VERIFY item) or add the independent confirmation (pulse-shape/decay-time difference between corner pairs) before submission.

Citations: PDG statistics review (scale factors), Efron/Tibshirani (paired bootstrap), a robust-statistics reference for truncated-RMS estimators, CMS MTD TDR and LHCb/TORCH-class timing papers for the 30 ps 4D context, and C. Hu et al. NIM A 954 (2020) for LuAG:Ce.
paper3_energy_position: RESOLVE THE POSITION ARITHMETIC FIRST: the quoted ~1.5 mm capillary-vs-WC residual at 150 GeV is BELOW the WC's own measured ~3.6 mm resolution - for independent detectors the event-by-event residual must satisfy sigma_res^2 = sigma_cap^2 + sigma_WC^2 >= sigma_WC^2, so 1.5 mm is arithmetically impossible unless (a) the residual was read off a binned ProfileX (centroid-of-bin averaging suppresses WC noise - a binned-centroid artifact, not a resolution), or (b) the 3.6 mm self-cal WC number is overestimated (the ~3.3 mm sub-sample CFD variant suggests it is soft). A referee will compute this in 30 seconds. Required: the event-by-event residual distribution, an explicit quadrature decomposition with the WC term, and presentation of capillary resolution as an upper limit or via a closure test - never as 'we localize to 1.5 mm'.

Estimator design with train/test separation: the TLinearFitter on 3 corner light-fractions has fitted coefficients - train on one run-fold, evaluate residuals OOS on the other. Compare the binned-centroid estimator to an unbinned per-event (likelihood/template) estimator built from the corner-fraction response vs (x,y); show the linearity S-curve (estimator vs WC truth, slope and edge bias) and a bias map across the fiducial. Respect the documented binning guardrail: position-dependent plots binned >= 3.5-4 mm.

Energy-resolution fits done to statistical standard: per-energy SumLG Gaussian fits shown (per-point transparency, as in published Figs 14-16) with stated fit-range convention and chi2; the 3-parameter a/sqrtE + b/E + c fit on only 5-6 energy points is barely constrained - report the full parameter error matrix and correlations, or fix the noise term and profile. Propagate the H2 beam momentum spread (~1% literature, <=2% max; flagged unmeasured-run-specific in VERIFY) explicitly - at 14% measured width it is small but must be shown, since it attacks the constant term.

Energy systematics rows: fiducial radius (2.0 mm nominal, swept), per-channel LG inter-calibration (the published paper equalized 8 channels to a common Gaussian peak - Fig 14; document the equalization and vary it), Pb-glass containment-cut threshold (0.30 swept), longitudinal-leakage/droop correction (9.2 -> 2.2% with Pb-glass linearization - quote the residual as a systematic), and run-period splits.

E-type characterization with honest framing: linearity fit (1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV) with residuals shown, per-cap sigma_E/E explicitly labeled constant-term dominated, and the statement that ONE E-type capillary in ONE build (TENERGY; no four-E-type configuration exists in 2023 data) is a characterization, not a resolution demonstration of the Fig-28 enhanced design.

4D-demo statistical consistency: the three panels currently use a scale-adaptive truncated RMS (coreS) while Paper 2's timing uses Gaussian-core tebSigma - a referee will flag two sigma definitions across companion papers. Re-extract all three with the documented tebSigma convention (robust fallback included), annotate N, and carry the co-registration systematics (module center +/-0.15 mm, MCP-RADiCAL alignment 0.79 mm, WC 3.6 mm) into the position panel caption. Label sigma_x = 0.9 mm as comparator-limited, same as the localization result.

OOS validation of every optimized choice, same table format as Paper 2: energy fiducial radius, the corner-fraction estimator coefficients, the per-energy adopted fit ranges - in-sample vs fold-OOS, one row each.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: A genuine single-module 4D (x, y, E, t) DEMONSTRATION at EM shower max: the TENERGY 150 GeV sample yields, from the same events in one module with a co-registered frame, sigma_t ~ 35-57 ps ((DW-UP)/2, 3 T-type caps), sigma_E/E ~ 12-14% (shower-max LG sum), and a transverse localization residual <~1.5 mm that is tracker-comparator-limited. All four observables are simultaneously measured per event - that is an honest, defensible 4D-calorimetry demonstration, with the explicit qualifiers 'shower-max sampling energy' and 'position upper limit set by the wire chamber'.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: 5D as a measured capability, and 4D 'performance at program goals'. The depth coordinate currently exists only as a self-consistency argument (sigma of (DW-UP)/2 x v_fiber ~ 4-5.6 mm ~ 1 X0 of shower-max wander) - there is no independent per-event depth truth in the data, so z is uncalibrated as a coordinate. Worse, t and z are the SAME observable read two ways: claiming both as independent dimensions double-counts unless a depth-independent absolute-time estimator (mean hg_led, which still needs the reducer-level timebase fix) is shown delivering t while (DW-UP)/2 delivers z. Also cannot claim: 1.5 mm as a calibrated position resolution (WC-limited), full-module energy resolution (Eq-1 scope fence), or that the 10%/sqrtE / 10 ps / few-mm program targets are met.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Two analyses, no new hardware. (1) In-data depth-axis calibration: the MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 must migrate with ln(E) because shower-max depth grows ~X0 per e-fold of energy (~+1 X0 over 25->150 GeV ~ 27 ps shift at v_fiber=200 mm/ns). Plot mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln E across the six DSB1 energies and compare slope to the GEANT4 shower-max depth migration (the paper's own Fig 7 simulation already shows the layer shift): agreement calibrates the z axis with data, converting the 'timing floor' into a measured 5th coordinate and licensing a (x, y, z_max, E, t) 5D claim with stated sigma_z ~ 4-5 mm. (2) Finish the depth-independent absolute shower time (mean hg_led + stop-cell correction, the depth_corr Paper-2 entry point) so t and z are demonstrably separable observables, closing the double-counting objection.

killer_additions: The in-data 5D calibration figure: mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) overlaid on the GEANT4 shower-max depth migration (the published paper's own Fig 7 predicts it). If the measured mean tracks the predicted ~X0-per-e-fold slope, the depth axis is calibrated from data alone - the floor stops being a limitation and becomes the fifth coordinate. This is a one-macro analysis on existing reduced data and would be the most-cited figure of the pair of papers.

A 'pre-registered pipeline' methods exemplar: one table per paper listing every optimized analysis choice with its in-sample and matched-fraction out-of-sample value side by side (including the deliberately reported in-sample-vs-OOS gap of the equal-width best-bin argmin, 25.6 -> 27.5 ps, which retroactively validates the published 27.4). Almost no test-beam paper does this; it makes the papers referee-proof and methodologically citable beyond RADiCAL.

A paired-inference statistical appendix: every 'method A beats B on identical events' statement (lgcfd vs cfd05 +3.1 ps; led vs cfd05 on LuAG; the MIXED in-event DSB1-vs-LuAG ratio 0.99) quoted with a paired event-bootstrap CI and a run-jackknife cross-check. The MIXED kill-shot with a proper confidence interval becomes an airtight, quotable result instead of a point estimate with a suspiciously small chi2.
referee_risks: Position arithmetic: 1.5 mm residual vs a 3.6 mm wire-chamber resolution is impossible for independent event-by-event measurements - if this reached print uncorrected it would draw an erratum-level objection. Resolve (binned-centroid artifact vs overestimated WC sigma) before any localization claim.

MIXED kill-shot circularity: corner material identities are data-inferred from brightness, and the thesis is then 'brightness, not species' - confirm the map against the logbook (open VERIFY item) or add an independent material discriminant, or expect the referee to find it.

Floor overclaiming: 'material-independent ~20 ps floor' rests on LuAG b = 19.8 +/- 5.6 ps (consistent with almost anything) while LuAG's best measured point is ~42 ps; and chi2/ndf ~ 2 on the adopted fits means errors must be PDG-scaled. State the floor as a per-technique extrapolation with measured-headline-first wording, exactly per the floor-honesty rules.

Tail-veto sculpting: the new in-event channel-consistency veto and skew/kurtosis guard could be read as 'cutting until Gaussian' - publish vetoed fractions vs E and a veto-threshold sweep, and show the published cfd05 numbers are unchanged by the fix.

Selection-of-best-estimator bias inherited from the published paper: any per-build 'winning' source or bin must be shown OOS-stable, or a methods referee will tag the multi-method survey as a garden of forking paths.

Cross-build confounds: the four builds are different run periods/weeks (DSB1 runs 1034-1333 vs MIXED 2787-3044) with different beam tunes and the MIXED restricted era - cross-build sigma_t and sigma_E comparisons need the run-period systematic row and the in-event MIXED comparison as the confound-free anchor.
Beam momentum spread is a literature value (~1%, <=2% max), not measured run-specific - it must be carried as a bounded systematic in the energy paper, not asserted.
Inconsistent sigma conventions across the two papers (Gaussian-core tebSigma vs truncated-RMS coreS in the 4D demo) - unify or explicitly cross-validate the two estimators on one sample.

experts.lens: Scientific language and framing: titles, claim calibration, honest caveats, estimator nomenclature, and how to phrase the WLS/light-yield thesis and the 4D/5D positioning without overclaiming.
paper1_assessment: [+] The energy framing is exemplary and must be inherited: the title says 'prospects for energy resolution', and Sec. 5.1.3 immediately after Eq. (1) states the 52.04%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31% result 'does not represent the energy resolution... of an EM shower in which all 29 LYSO:Ce layers... would contribute' but only the ~3-tile shower-max sampling. This pre-empts the obvious attack (9.31% constant term vs the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% goal) and is the template for every follow-up energy sentence.

[+] Floor honesty in the conclusion wording is correctly conditional: the abstract and Sec. 6 headline the MEASURED 27 ps at 150 GeV, and the 17.5 ps asymptote is quoted as 'in the limit of high energy... under the current measurement conditions'. The satellite artifact is disclosed rather than hidden (Fig. 19, ~15% area, ~0.2 ns shift, 'has little effect'). Follow-ups should keep exactly this measured-first / extrapolation-second structure.

[-] 'Best estimator' / 'BestMinus' / 'BestPlus' is self-congratulatory, non-descriptive jargon. 'BestMinus' encodes nothing about what is computed (the (Delta_t_DW - Delta_t_UP)/2 restricted to amplitude bins 6-8 of Figs. 21-26); Fig. 17 even labels ENERGY points 'Best Estimator', where 'best' is meaningless. The six methods are defined in numbered prose (Sec. 6, items 1-6) scattered over two pages with no formula table, and the value judgment 'best' is baked into the name a referee can contest. The follow-ups need a neutral, descriptive nomenclature plus an explicit old-name -> new-name mapping.

[-] The defining selection of the headline ('bins 6-8', i.e. events where measured energy ~ nominal) is described only as assuring the resolution 'is determined when the observed energy... corresponds very closely to the actual delivered beam energy' - argmin-adjacent language with no statement of bin construction (equal-width vs equal-population) and no out-of-sample defense. Session work shows equal-width best-bin is precisely the one selection that genuinely overfits (the published value happens to be the honest OOS number, but the paper never argues it). Follow-ups must replace this with a deterministic, stated brightest-fraction selection and say why it cannot overfit.

[-] The central technical fact is buried in one clause: 'the high gain signals were driven further into saturation, to maximize the signal rise' (Sec. 4) - with no clip level (~820 mV, a DT5742 range clip, not SiPM saturation), no clipped fraction (71-94% above 50 GeV), and no discussion of the time-walk cost of a fixed threshold on a clipped pulse. A reader cannot evaluate the fixed-threshold LED choice, and the motivation for any edge-recovery successor method is invisible.

[-] No uncertainties or systematics language anywhere on the timing result: Eq. (2) quotes a=256, b=17.5 with no errors, no fit quality, no selection-variation budget. The MCP reference is asserted as '10 ps < sigma_t < 20 ps' while the only in-data reference figure, sigma(MCP1-MCP2)/sqrt2 ~ 71-74 ps, is actually inter-group DRS4 domino-phase jitter - the paper's 'unsubtracted resolution contribution due to the MCP' (BestPlus-MCP, ~10 ps) conflates two different effects and leaves the why-the-differential-estimator-wins story untold.
paper2_timing: TITLE (proposal): 'Comparison of DSB1 and LuAG:Ce wavelength shifters for precision shower-maximum timing in an ultra-compact W/LYSO:Ce sampling calorimeter (RADiCAL) module at 25-150 GeV'. Open Sec. 1 with the promise-kept sentence: 'This paper presents the comparative DSB1 vs LuAG:Ce timing study announced in Sec. 7 of [NIM A 1068 (2024) 169737]'. Subtitle/running theme: light yield as the dominant lever.

ABSTRACT SKELETON: (1) one sentence of module invariants - 'W and LYSO:Ce are common to all configurations; only the wavelength-shifting capillary fill differs'; (2) the four builds (DSB1, LuAG:Ce, mixed in-module, 3T+1E) at SPS H2, 25/50-150 GeV; (3) method sentence - a low-gain-anchored constant-fraction discriminator recovers the rising edge lost to digitizer-range clipping, removing the ~0.2 ns satellite documented in [parent]; (4) headline MEASURED numbers - DSB1 25.3 ps at 150 GeV (brightest-1000), LuAG 42 ps; sigma_t = a/sqrtE (+) b with a tracking light yield (x2.3) and b ~ 20 ps shared across builds, CONSISTENT WITH the published 17.5 ps; (5) the kill-shot - within one mixed module the two WLS species time identically (per-capillary ratio 0.99), so the build ordering is attributable to light yield; (6) scope-limited conclusion: 'light yield, rather than WLS species, governs the timing performance of this module geometry'.

NOMENCLATURE TABLE (Table 1, mandatory): retire BestMinus/BestPlus. Define t_diff = (t_DW - t_UP)/2 'dual-end half-difference (reference-free)' and t_sum = (t_DW + t_UP)/2 'dual-end mean time (MCP-referenced)'; name timing sources by mechanism - 'fixed-threshold leading edge (LED, 20 mV)', 'constant fraction of the recorded peak (CFD-f)', and rename lgcfd to something physical, e.g. 'saturation-recovered CFD (srCFD)' or 'LG-anchored CFD': CFD at f of the LG-PREDICTED true peak. Columns: name | formula | reference dependence | what cancels (MCP jitter, domino phase, common-mode edge shape) | role. Include the explicit mapping row: 'the estimator called BestMinus in [parent] corresponds to the amplitude-selected t_diff here'.

CLAIM LADDER for the thesis (three tiers, in this order): (i) OBSERVED - the stochastic term a orders by light yield across builds; (ii) CONFOUND NAMED IN PRINT - a between-build comparison cannot separate light level from WLS decay kinetics (LuAG slower; CTR ratio ~ light ratio); (iii) RESOLUTION - the in-module mixed comparison (same showers, same LYSO:Ce, same DRS group) gives per-capillary sigma ratio 0.99 (chi2/ndf 0.4/5). Adopted verb: light yield 'governs' / 'is the dominant lever' - never 'WLS species is irrelevant', and never the bare 'sets, not species'. Scope-limit: one module geometry, one beamline.

FLOOR LANGUAGE (the fatal-if-wrong section): headline the MEASURED 25.3 ps at 150 GeV; present b as a PER-TECHNIQUE, FORM-DEPENDENT EXTRAPOLATION beyond the top beam energy - show both 1/sqrtE and 1/E forms with chi2 per build, quote b with stat (+) syst (DSB1 19.5 +/- 1.1 +/- 1.2 ps); the verb for 17.5 ps is 'consistent with / confirms', NEVER 'revises'. State plainly that LuAG's brightest 150 GeV point is still ~42 ps and never reaches the extrapolated floor in data. Frame the floor identity as a PREDICTION the data confirms: sigma_z = v_fiber * sigma_t ~ 4-5.6 mm ~ 1 X0 of shower-max fluctuation, hence material-independent by geometry.

METHOD-GAIN FRAMING: the recovered-edge CFD is 'the enabling method, not the result' - +3.1 ps at 150 GeV and floor 22.1 -> 19.5 ps on IDENTICAL events, stochastic term unchanged (205 -> 201). Demonstrate, do not assert, the satellite removal (cfd05-vs-srCFD distribution overlay), citing the parent's Fig. 19 satellite as the documented artifact being cured. Also state the documented cfd05 failure modes per regime: time-walk on the brightest clipped events (motivates srCFD) and noise-dominated 5% crossings on dim pulses (motivates LED for LuAG/E-type) - this is why the per-build source choice is principled, not tuned.

MIXED-MODULE WORDING: state up front that a single module-wide estimator is ILL-POSED in a mixed-light module (two corners clip, two do not - no one source is valid for all eight ends); the defined observable is per-material diagonals within the same showers. Frame this as a feature of the design ('the mixed module is the controlled experiment'), not an analysis fallback. Flag the corner-material map provenance honestly (data-inferred brightness matching; confirm against the logbook before submission or describe the inference).

VALIDATION LANGUAGE REQUIRED: (a) a systematics table with the explicit sentence that the timing-source choice is EXCLUDED because it is the measurand, not a nuisance; (b) an out-of-sample paragraph - 'the brightest-fraction selection is deterministic and fold-stable; the equal-width best-bin argmin of [parent] is shown to coincide with its out-of-sample value, so the published 27 ps stands as the prior'; (c) one paragraph naming the 71-74 ps MCP1-MCP2 figure as inter-group domino-phase jitter that cancels exactly in same-group t_diff - this corrects the parent's MCP conflation without criticizing it.

CITATIONS: parent NIM A 1068 (2024) 169737 (baseline + Sec. 7 promise); C. Hu et al., NIM A 954 (2020) 161723 (LuAG:Ce); LuAG/LYSO decay-kinetics reference for the confound paragraph; CMS MTD TDR and CMS HGCAL TDR + FCC-hh refs for the quantitative 20-30 ps context (PU ~ 1000); DRS4/DT5742 references for the domino-jitter and clipping discussion; the parent's Ref. 22 (Cates et al.) for the HG amplifier design.
paper3_energy_position: TITLE (proposal): 'Shower-maximum energy response, transverse shower localization, and a simultaneous time-energy-position measurement in an ultra-compact W/LYSO:Ce (RADiCAL) calorimeter module'. Open with the promise-kept sentence: 'This paper presents the EM shower localization study, correlating capillary energy information with the beamline wire chamber, announced in Sec. 7 of [NIM A 1068]'.

ABSTRACT SKELETON: (1) module invariants + builds; (2) define the named quantity ONCE - 'the shower-maximum sampled energy E_SM, the light collected from ~3 LYSO:Ce tiles (~1 R_M radial slice) at shower max'; (3) E_SM linear to 150 GeV, sigma_E_SM/E ~ 14% at 150 GeV and WLS-species-independent (13.5-15% across builds); (4) four-corner amplitude localization: residual 1.5 mm vs the wire chamber, comparator-limited; (5) first in-beam characterization of the full-length E-type capillary (linear, ~4-5x lower light, uniform sampling); (6) capstone: one module measures arrival time, E_SM, and transverse position simultaneously for the same events - a four-dimensional (x, y, E, t) shower-maximum measurement.

ENERGY CAVEAT, SYSTEMATIZED: inherit the parent's Sec. 5.1.3 caveat but make it structural, not parenthetical - introduce the E_SM symbol in Sec. 2 and tag EVERY resolution as sigma_E_SM/E. One sentence per use of '14%': 'this is the resolution of a localized shower-max sampling, not the module energy resolution; the large constant term is restricted transverse sampling, not a detector defect'. Never let an abstract or conclusion sentence carry an energy-resolution number without the E_SM qualifier - that single omission is the most likely referee attack.

POSITION GUARDRAIL WORDING (the 1.5 mm < 3.6 mm paradox): the wire chamber's own resolution is ~3.6 mm, so 1.5 mm CANNOT be quoted as 'the position resolution'. Precise honest phrasing: 'the four-corner amplitude estimator reproduces the wire-chamber coordinate with an RMS residual of 1.5 mm (1.52 mm in x, 1.44 mm in y) at 150 GeV; since the comparator is not independently better than the estimator, this residual is an upper bound on the capillary localization and we do not unfold a standalone capillary resolution'. Quote the program goal as met in the form stated: 'localization within a few mm'. Add the domain caveat: demonstrated over the ~2.9 mm beam-spot footprint near module center, not the full module face; and the binning guardrail (position-dependent analyses binned >= 3.5-4 mm).

E-TYPE FRAMING: 'first in-beam characterization of the full-length energy capillary' - linear (1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV), ~4-5x lower light at shower max, uniform longitudinal sampling - explicitly 'the in-beam analogue of [parent] Fig. 4'. Division-of-labour sentence: 'the T-type/E-type role split is set by light-collection geometry and filament placement, not by WLS species'. State that the E-type is excluded from the corner timing estimator and why.

4D CAPSTONE WORDING: 'a simultaneous measurement of arrival time, shower-maximum sampled energy, and transverse position from a single event sample in one module' - sigma_t ~ 57 ps all-fiducial (~35 ps brightest selection), sigma_E_SM/E ~ 11.9%, 0.9 mm residual vs tracker, at 150 GeV. Call it 'a demonstration of the four-dimensional measurement principle', NEVER 'a 4D calorimeter meeting the program goals' (the demo numbers are not the 10%/sqrtE / <=20 ps / few-mm goal numbers, and one of the three is comparator-limited). Co-registration sentence: shared frame via module center (6.58, 4.66 mm), MCP-module alignment 0.79 mm, tracker resolution 3.6 mm.

DEPTH BRIDGE SECTION (the Paper2<->Paper3 hinge): one section stating that t_diff = (t_DW - t_UP)/2 is simultaneously the timing floor (Paper 2) and a longitudinal depth coordinate (this paper): sigma_z = v_fiber * sigma_t with v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns gives 4-5.6 mm ~ 1 X0, consistent with shower-max fluctuation - 'the same observable read two ways'. This is also where the route below the ~20 ps floor (depth-corrected absolute time) is framed as future work, keeping Paper 2's floor claim intact.

NUMERICAL HYGIENE REQUIRED IN PROSE: leakage discussion (LG sum droop 9-20% at 150 GeV; Pb-glass correction linearizes 9.2% -> 2.2%); the containment/event-class taxonomy (good-EM vs punch-through vs halo) justifying the 30% Pb-glass cut; the beam momentum spread stated AS the H2 literature value (~1%, <=2%), explicitly labeled not run-measured; per-point uncertainties on every sigma_E_SM/E and residual figure (the parent's Fig. 17 had error bars - do not regress).

fourD_verdict.can_claim: A demonstrated FOUR-dimensional (x, y, E, t) measurement at EM shower maximum in a single ultra-compact module: the TENERGY 150 GeV sample yields, from the same events, sigma_t ~ 57 ps all-fiducial (~35 ps brightest selection), sigma_E_SM/E ~ 11.9% (shower-max sampled), and a 0.9-1.5 mm transverse residual against the beamline tracker. Honest headline phrasing: 'a simultaneous time-energy-position (4D) shower-maximum measurement in one device'. Additionally claimable: demonstrated SENSITIVITY to the fifth coordinate - longitudinal depth - because (t_DW - t_UP)/2 is a depth coordinate by construction and its measured spread (sigma_z = v_fiber * sigma_t ~ 4-5.6 mm) is consistent with the expected ~1 X0 shower-max fluctuation.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Not '5D calorimetry' as a measured resolution: there is no independent per-event depth truth in this dataset, so the instrumental depth resolution and the genuine shower-depth fluctuation are degenerate in the one measured number - a standalone sigma_z(instrumental) cannot be quoted. Also not claimable: a 4D detector 'meeting program goals' (57 ps and 11.9% E_SM are demonstration numbers, not the <=20 ps / 10%/sqrtE goals); a calibrated standalone position resolution (comparator-limited at 3.6 mm, demonstrated only over the ~2.9 mm beam-spot domain); and full-shower 4D (energy is shower-max sampled only).
fourD_verdict.unlock: One cheap, decisive analysis: plot the MEAN of (t_DW - t_UP)/2 versus beam energy (six DSB1 points, 25-150 GeV) converted to depth via v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns, and overlay the GEANT4 shower-max depth vs energy (the parent's own Fig. 7 shows shower max moving from layers 8-10 to 11-13). If the measured mean tracks the predicted ~ln(E) depth drift, the depth coordinate is CALIBRATED in-data, not asserted - licensing the upgraded claim 'demonstrated and calibrated longitudinal (depth) sensitivity, toward 5D shower-maximum calorimetry'. A supporting GEANT4 sigma_z(E) comparison would further bound the instrumental-vs-fluctuation split. Both reuse existing reduced branches; no new data needed.

killer_additions: THE PROMISE-KEPT DEVICE: open each follow-up with a half-page roadmap table mapping NIM A 1068 Sec. 7 items to delivered results ('item 2 -> this paper; item 1 -> companion paper'), and brand the manuscripts as a series (RADiCAL II: Timing; RADiCAL III: Energy and Localization). Referees and citers reward a kept roadmap, and the series framing makes the parent's caveats (E_SM, floor conditions) inherited context rather than re-litigated claims.

A CITABLE NOTATION STANDARD: one shared Table 1 in both papers that retires BestMinus/BestPlus for good - t_diff/t_sum plus mechanism-named sources (LED, CFD-f, saturation-recovered CFD), each with formula, reference dependence, and an explicit what-cancels column (MCP jitter, DRS4 domino phase, common-mode edge shape), plus the old-name mapping row to [parent]. This single table converts house jargon into adoptable community nomenclature - the kind of methodological clarity that makes a NIM paper the standard reference for dual-end shower-max timing.

THE DEPTH-CALIBRATION FIGURE: mean (t_DW - t_UP)/2 vs E overlaid on GEANT4 shower-max depth (extending the parent's Fig. 7), one figure that simultaneously (a) proves the timing floor IS shower-depth physics rather than an instrumental excuse, (b) calibrates the fifth coordinate in-data, and (c) gives the series its memorable closing line: 'the floor of the timing measurement is the signal of the depth measurement'. No competing compact-calorimeter result can currently make that statement.
referee_risks: Any sentence reading as a quiet revision of the published 17.5 ps floor (e.g. headlining b ~ 19.5-25 ps) - collaboration-internal and external referees will both object; the only safe verbs are 'consistent with' and 'confirms', with the floor explicitly per-technique and an extrapolation beyond 150 GeV.

Quoting '1.5 mm position resolution' when the wire-chamber comparator is ~3.6 mm - an immediate, justified objection; must be worded as a comparator-limited residual/upper bound, with the >= 3.5-4 mm binning guardrail and the beam-spot (~2.9 mm central footprint) domain caveat stated.

The light-yield-not-species claim resting on between-build comparison alone - the LuAG decay-kinetics confound (CTR ratio ~ light ratio ~ 2.3-2.6x) is real and known; the claim must be carried by the in-module MIXED kill-shot, with the caveat that even it operates at the ~200 ps per-capillary level and the cross-build floor equality (LuAG b = 19.8 +/- 5.6 ps) is extrapolation-weak (LuAG never measures below ~42 ps).

Selection-tuning suspicion ('did you pick the bins/cuts that flatter you?') - inherited from the parent's undefended 'bins 6-8' language; defused only by the deterministic brightest-fraction definition, the OOS fold paragraph, the systematics table, and the optimization-landscape appendix showing knee/plateau working points.

MIXED corner-material map is data-inferred, flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook in memory - a referee asking 'how do you know NE/SW are DSB1?' needs either the logbook citation or the published inference method; do not present the map as established without one.

Energy-resolution numbers escaping their E_SM qualifier anywhere (abstract, conclusion, figure caption) - instantly draws 'this is nowhere near 10%/sqrtE'; also the ~9-20% high-energy LG droop and Pb-glass correction must be discussed or the linearity claim is attackable.

4D/5D inflation: claiming '4D calorimeter' (vs '4D measurement demonstration at shower max'), or '5D' without an independent depth reference; and conflating the TENERGY demo numbers (57 ps, 11.9%) with the program goals (<= 20 ps, 10%/sqrtE).

MCP-reference language: asserting a 10-20 ps reference while the in-data MCP1-MCP2 spread is 71-74 ps invites 'your reference is worse than claimed' - print the inter-group domino-phase explanation and why same-group differentials cancel it exactly.
Unmeasured beam momentum spread (~1% H2 literature value) silently absorbed into sigma_E or low-energy sigma_t - label it as a literature value and propagate it explicitly at 25 GeV.

Adopting different timing sources per build (srCFD for bright, LED for dim) can read as per-build tuning - it must be framed as a documented, regime-driven choice (clipping time-walk vs noise-floor crossings) with the all-sources survey figure showing the adopted source dominates the alternatives for every build.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: 4D yes, with disciplined wording: the TENERGY 150 GeV demo (fourD_demo.png) shows ONE module measuring time ((DW-UP)/2 over 3 T-types, ~57 ps all-fiducial / ~35 ps best-bin), shower-max energy (8-LG sum, sigma_E/E ~11.9%), and transverse position (4-corner light vs WC, 0.9 mm residual) SIMULTANEOUSLY from the same event set -- 'simultaneous 4D (x,y,E,t) reconstruction demonstrated in a single ultracompact module'. Data-only; no new simulation required for this sentence. A 5D OBSERVABLE also already exists in the data: (DW-UP)/2 IS a longitudinal depth estimator by construction (2 ps/mm at v~200 mm/ns), and its ENSEMBLE behavior (floor sigma_z ~ 4-5 mm ~ 1 X0) is consistent with shower-depth fluctuation.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Cannot claim program targets met in 4D (10 ps / 1 mm / 10%/sqrtE are all unmet; energy is shower-max sampling at 52%/sqrtE+9.3%, not full-module). Cannot claim 0.9 or 1.5 mm as a CALIBRATED position resolution -- the wire-chamber comparator itself is ~3.6 mm, so the residual is comparator-limited. Cannot yet claim '5D calorimetry': there is no demonstrated EVENT-BY-EVENT depth measurement, only ensemble consistency; 'the floor equals shower-depth fluctuation' is currently a back-of-envelope (sigma_z = v*sigma_t ~ 1 X0) with no truth-level validation, and the naive 1 X0 number ignores the 15-mm filament acceptance-window truncation.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Three cheap unlocks. (1) DATA-ONLY ensemble test: the mean of (DW-UP)/2 must DRIFT with energy as shower max migrates -- Geant4/PDG predicts Delta z ~ X0*ln(150/25) ~ 9.7 mm -> ~48 ps mean shift from 25 to 150 GeV at 2 ps/mm; measuring this drift (DSB1, 6 energies, already in the reduced trees) is a free truth-level validation that the observable tracks depth. (2) DATA-ONLY event-level test: correlate the two INDEPENDENT depth observables per event -- (DW-UP)/2 timing vs the UP/DW amplitude ratio; pure noise would decorrelate, so the shared covariance directly measures genuine per-event depth resolution (the in-event analog of the MIXED kill-shot). (3) SIM (Stage-1, energy-deposition only, no optical photons): event-by-event light-weighted true depth through the measured Fig-4 acceptance kernel A(z), regressed against both observables -> a ps-to-mm depth calibration and a predicted sigma_z(E). With (1)+(2)+(3) the paper can honestly claim 'per-event shower-depth sensitivity, sigma_z ~ 4-5 mm ~ 1 X0' -- evidence-based 5D -- and the same analysis is the depth-corrected route below the 20 ps floor.

killer_additions: THE SIMULATED TWIN OF THE MONEY PLOT: one Geant4-parameterized sigma_t(Npe; E) surface -- Stage-1 Edep + measured Fig-4 acceptance kernel + photostatistics term anchored per build via the fitted stochastic a -- on which all FOUR builds (DSB1/LuAG/MIXED/TENERGY) lie as four Npe values on one curve, with the depth-fluctuation floor emerging naturally as the Npe->infinity asymptote at ~18-20 ps. One figure that simultaneously confirms the light-yield thesis in silico, decomposes the 17.5 ps floor, and explains why more light cannot beat it. This is what elevates Paper 2 from 'careful beam-test comparison' to 'understood detector'.

THE DATA-ONLY 5D DEMONSTRATOR: (a) measured mean drift of (DW-UP)/2 vs beam energy matching the Geant4/PDG X0*ln(E) shower-max migration (~48 ps over 25-150 GeV), plus (b) the per-event correlation between timing-based depth and UP/DW amplitude-ratio depth whose covariance extracts the true per-event depth resolution. Costs essentially nothing beyond Stage-1 sim, uses branches already in the reduced trees, and converts the 4D demo into the first evidence-based 5D shower-max calorimetry claim -- the memorable result of Paper 3.

THE ENERGY-EQUIVALENT CALIBRATION OF SHOWER-MAX SAMPLING: publish, for the first time, what fraction of the shower (in GeV) the ~3-tile/1-R_M shower-max measurement actually samples, and decompose the measured 52%/sqrtE + 31.6%/E + 9.3% into Geant4 sampling-fraction fluctuation + photostatistics + (Pb-glass-validated) transverse leakage. Turns an empirical mV-space fit into a calorimetry-physics result, substantiates the claim that the 9.3% constant term is transverse acceptance (not longitudinal leakage from the 25 X0 stack), and quantifies the real distance to the 10%/sqrtE program goal for the 18x18 Fig-28 module.
experts.lens: GEANT4 simulation (shower physics, optical transport, data-MC validation strategy)
paper1_assessment: [+] Fig 7 (longitudinal Edep/E0 per LYSO layer at 20/50/125 GeV) is honest, load-bearing simulation: it grounds the T-type filament placement AND openly flags that shower max migrates from layers 8-10 (FTBF-era optimization) to 11-13 at 100-150 GeV -- a concrete, checkable data-MC hook the follow-ups can cash in (the (DW-UP)/2 mean must drift with E).

[+] Fig 9 (transverse energy map at shower max, r <= 5 mm << R_M = 13.7 mm) plus Fig 4 (bench LED scan of T/E-type longitudinal response) are strategically placed simulation/bench seeds: Fig 9 motivates the module-ID/localization program of Paper 3, and Fig 4 is, unappreciated by the paper itself, a MEASURED longitudinal acceptance kernel A(z) that can replace a full optical Monte Carlo in every follow-up.

[-] Fig 8 (sigma_t vs LY in npe/MeV, 50 GeV) simulates the WRONG estimator and floats unanchored: it models only single-ended downstream readout -- the adopted BestMinus (DW-UP)/2 differential is never simulated -- its npe/MeV axis is never connected to the measured light scale (so the simulated curve cannot be quantitatively compared to the measured 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps), and its provenance is an indico talk [ref 12] with no physics list, optical model, version, or cuts stated. Irreproducible by a reader.

[-] The 17.5 ps floor (Eq 2) is left physically undecomposed -- no simulated shower-depth-fluctuation contribution, despite (DW-UP)/2 being a depth estimator by construction; 'in the limit of high energy, the timing resolution is 17.5 ps under the current measurement conditions' invites the referee question the paper cannot answer (what IS the floor?).

[-] The energy result is purely empirical in mV-space: 29.4 mV/GeV and Eq 1 (52.04%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31%) with no Geant4 sampling-fraction calibration -- the reader cannot tell intrinsic shower-max sampling fluctuation from photostatistics from leakage, and the section-3 promise that distribution-shape factors 'were considered in the simulation discussion' is never delivered as a simulated E_meas distribution.

[-] The admitted filament mispositioning at high energy (Fig 7 caption: 'adequate (although not optimized)... will be corrected in future work') is never quantified -- no estimate of the light-collection or timing penalty at 100-150 GeV, leaving the high-E end of both Eq 1 and Eq 2 exposed.
paper2_timing: NECESSARY -- the floor-decomposition simulation (the one simulation Paper 2 cannot be world-class without). Stage-1 Geant4, ENERGY DEPOSITION ONLY (no optical photons): exact stack (29 x 1.5 mm LYSO / 28 x 2.5 mm W, Tyvek gaps, quartz capillaries in the corner holes), 25-150 GeV e-, beam spot 2.9 mm + ~1% Delta p/p, fiducial r matched to SelectionCuts. Per event, compute the light-weighted mean depth z-bar by folding Edep(z) in LYSO with the MEASURED Fig-4 T-type acceptance kernel A(z) (15 mm filament span) and a transverse proximity weight to each corner capillary; sigma(z-bar)/v_g -> predicted (DW-UP)/2 floor vs E. Overlay the predicted band on the measured per-build b ~ 18-20 ps and published 17.5 ps. This converts 'sigma_z = v*sigma_t ~ 1 X0, numbers happen to match' into a validated decomposition, correctly includes the acceptance-window truncation and discrete-layer sampling the back-of-envelope ignores, and PREDICTS the floor's material-independence (geometry, not WLS) -- upgrading the thesis from observation to prediction. ~10k events x 6 energies suffices.

NECESSARY -- a written, justified optical PARAMETERIZATION in place of full optical transport, with one targeted validation. Model: t_end = t_dep + z/v_g + common WLS-decay draw (tau = 3.5 ns, COMMON to both ends -> cancels in DW-UP except its depth-correlated part) + per-end photostatistics jitter. Justify v_g from the fused-silica group index (~204 mm/ns) against the measured ~200 mm/ns; bound modal path-length dispersion for the 0.95 mm core analytically. Validate with a Stage-2 SINGLE-CAPILLARY G4 optical run (quartz core + WLS emission at 495 nm + silica attenuation + HDR2 PDE window, ~1e5 photons per emission point, scan z) whose z-response must reproduce the published Fig-4 bench curve -- a free data anchor. Then state explicitly in the paper why full-module optical Monte Carlo is NOT required for a differential estimator. Referees punish both unjustified parameterizations and gratuitous optical MCs; this threads the needle.

NECESSARY -- anchor the simulated sigma_t(Npe) to the measured light scale (the fix for the published Fig 8). Re-simulate the curve for the ACTUAL (DW-UP)/2 estimator, dual-ended; extract per-build effective Npe from the fitted stochastic terms (a = 201 DSB1 ... 455 LuAG, the x2.3 light ratio) and show the four builds slide along ONE simulated curve. This is the simulated twin of light_yield_thesis.png and the quantitative closure of Fig 8's dangling '<50 ps achievable depending on light level'.

NECESSARY -- the mean-drift cross-check (data + Fig-7 physics, zero new sim beyond Stage 1): shower max migrates ~X0*ln(E2/E1) ~ 9.7 mm from 25 to 150 GeV -> the MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 must shift ~48 ps across the scan (2 ps/mm). Measure it on DSB1 (6 energies); agreement validates the G4 longitudinal profile at truth level and simultaneously quantifies the admitted filament mispositioning (fold A(z) with the per-energy profile -> fractional light loss and any high-E bias of the floor). Also a Paper-3 5D enabler.

NECESSARY -- MIXED diagonal-equivalence check (cheap, Edep-only): show in G4 that after the per-run centroid + fiducial procedure the NE/SW and NW/SE diagonals sample statistically identical shower light (no geometric bias between diagonals), and that shower-time common-mode cancels in the same-layer pairwise solve. This is the simulation defense of the per-material-diagonal method and the 0.99 in-event kill-shot against 'the two diagonals see different showers'.

NECESSARY -- a reproducibility paragraph the published paper lacks: Geant4 version (e.g. 11.x), FTFP_BERT + G4EmStandardPhysics_option4 (EMZ) for shower profiles, region-based production cuts (~10-50 um in LYSO near the capillaries where sub-mm gradients matter), Birks quenching with a cited LYSO kB (and a statement that it is negligible for EM showers -- verified, not asserted), event counts, and a longitudinal-profile sanity check against the PDG gamma-profile t_max = ln(E/Ec) - 0.5. Cite this in place of the talk-reference [12].

CAN BE CLAIMED WITHOUT NEW SIMULATION: the light-yield thesis (money plot + MIXED 0.99 kill-shot), the lgcfd method gain (+3.1 ps, floor 22->19.5), the photostat 1/sqrtE form preference, the floor CONSISTENCY check sigma_z ~ 1 X0, and the satellite-removal demonstration -- all pure data. What data alone CANNOT support: 'the floor IS shower-depth fluctuation' as a decomposition (needs item 1 or at minimum the mean-drift check), and any extrapolated b below the 150 GeV reach.
paper3_energy_position: NECESSARY -- energy-equivalent calibration of the 52%/sqrtE shower-max sampling (Stage-1 Edep, no optics): per event, compute the GeV actually sampled by the ~3-tile/1-R_M acceptance (Edep folded with Fig-4 A(z) and the transverse capillary weights) -> publish the sampling fraction in physical units (the paper only has 29.4 mV/GeV) and the predicted sampled-fraction fluctuation. Decompose the measured sigma_E/E into (i) intrinsic sampling fluctuation (G4), (ii) LG photostatistics, (iii) leakage/halo -- and show the decomposition PREDICTS the measured WLS-species-INDEPENDENCE (13.5-15% across builds) because sampling, not light, dominates. This is what makes the energy half of the paper calorimetry rather than curve-fitting.

NECESSARY -- simulated position-resolution expectation vs the comparator-limited measurement: G4 transfer function of 4-corner light sharing (corner-weighted Edep vs true (x,y) across the fiducial), then propagate measured photostatistics + shower transverse fluctuations -> predicted sigma_x,y(E) from amplitude asymmetry. REQUIRED because the measured 1.5 mm (DSB1) / 0.9 mm (TENERGY) residuals sit BELOW the wire chamber's own ~3.6 mm resolution: without an MC expectation the paper can only say 'comparator-limited'; with it, the claim becomes 'G4 expects ~x mm intrinsic; the measured residual is WC-dominated and consistent' -- the only honest route toward the 'few mm' / 1 mm program-goal language. Respect the >=3.5-4 mm WC binning guardrail in every data-MC comparison.

NECESSARY -- leakage/containment validation (Stage-1): predict longitudinal leakage out of the 25 X0 stack (small) vs transverse leakage out of the ~1 R_M cross-section (dominant) vs E; compare to the measured 9-20% LG droop and the Pb-glass-tagged population fractions (Pop A-D, 6.2% punch-through at 150 GeV). This substantiates the currently asserted 'the 9.3% constant term is restricted transverse sampling, NOT longitudinal leakage' -- a sentence a calorimetry referee will absolutely test.

NECESSARY -- E-type characterization closed against simulation: fold the same per-energy Edep(z) with the uniform E-type kernel (Fig 4 blue) vs the localized T-type kernel (Fig 4 orange) -> predicted E/T amplitude ratio and slopes; match the measured 4-5x dimmer, 1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV with ONE free collection-efficiency parameter, and predict the E-type's flat, constant-term-dominated sigma_E/E. Confirms in-beam that the T/E division of labour is geometry+role, not species, and is the in-beam closure of the published Fig 4.

NECESSARY -- the 5D depth observable analysis (the paper's distinctive result; see verdict): (a) measured mean (DW-UP)/2 drift vs E against the G4 X0*ln(E) prediction (~48 ps over the scan), (b) per-event correlation of timing-depth vs UP/DW amplitude-ratio depth (covariance = true depth resolution), (c) Stage-1 truth regression of both observables against light-weighted z-bar -> ps-to-mm calibration and sigma_z(E). Also the quantitative basis for the depth-corrected absolute-time estimator (the route below the 20 ps floor) flagged as the Paper-3 entry point.

CAN BE CLAIMED WITHOUT NEW SIMULATION: sigma_E/E ~ 14% at 150 GeV and its species-independence; position residual <= 1.5 mm AS comparator-limited; the 4D simultaneity demo; E-type linearity and dimness as measured facts. CANNOT without sim: any GeV-language sampling fraction, any calibrated sub-mm position resolution, the leakage attribution of the constant term, and any per-event depth (5D) resolution number.

MINIMAL SIM CAMPAIGN (shared with Paper 2; total ~days of CPU): Stage 1 = Geant4 11.x, FTFP_BERT + EM option4 (EMZ), full module geometry incl. Tyvek and quartz capillaries, optical physics OFF, Birks ON (negligibility verified), beam 2.9 mm spot + 1% Delta p/p, region cuts 10-50 um near capillaries, ~10-50k e- x 6 energies, scoring: per-tile Edep, fine-z Edep, transverse maps at capillary radii, per-event z-bar through measured A(z). Serves B1/B4/B5, C1-C5, and D. Stage 2 = single-capillary G4 optical run (G4OpticalPhysics ON: quartz core, WLS 495 nm tau 3.5 ns, silica attenuation, HDR2 PDE) solely to extract v_g, path dispersion, capture fraction vs z, validated against the published Fig-4 bench scan. Explicitly DECLINE full-module optical Monte Carlo, with the differential-estimator justification written in the text.
referee_risks: Quoting 0.9/1.5 mm as a position RESOLUTION when the wire-chamber comparator is ~3.6 mm -- must be worded as comparator-limited residual or unfolded via the G4 expectation; also any position-dependent analysis binned finer than ~3.5-4 mm will be attacked as smearing-correlated.

Asserting 'the 17.5-20 ps floor is shower-depth fluctuation' from the v_fiber back-of-envelope alone: the naive ~1 X0 number ignores the 15-mm filament acceptance truncation, transverse weighting, and discrete LYSO sampling; without the Stage-1 decomposition (or at least the measured mean-drift cross-check) this is an unproven identification.

Floor b as extrapolation: no beam data above 150 GeV; LuAG's brightest measured point is still ~42 ps, never reaching its extrapolated ~20 ps floor; per-technique floors (cfd05 vs lgcfd, 4.2 sigma apart) must never be force-shared; headline the MEASURED 25.3-27 ps, quote b with stat+syst and the a-b anticorrelation.

Irreproducible simulation provenance: the published Fig 8 rests on a talk reference with no physics list, version, optical model, or npe anchoring, and simulates the wrong (single-ended) estimator -- repeating that pattern in Papers 2-3 will draw a methods objection; conversely a full-module optical MC would draw 'why, for a differential estimator?' -- justify the parameterization explicitly.

MIXED-specific attacks: the corner material map is data-inferred (CONFIRM against logbook before publication -- the NW inference is weakest); the per-material-diagonal method needs the G4 diagonal-equivalence check; MIXED's elevated 34.6 ps floor must be framed as a build feature, not hidden.

Energy claims: calling the 52%/sqrtE 'energy resolution' without the shower-max-sampling-only caveat (the published paper is careful here -- keep being careful); attributing the 9.3% constant term to transverse sampling without the leakage simulation; ignoring Birks/light-collection nonuniformity in the GeV-equivalent calibration.

The admitted filament mispositioning at 100-150 GeV (published Fig 7 caption): if unquantified, a referee can use it against both the high-E timing floor and the high-E energy points; the A(z) x profile fold neutralizes it.

Claiming '5D' on ensemble consistency alone: without the event-level two-observable depth correlation (or truth-level G4 regression), 'depth measurement' will be read as restating the timing floor; also avoid implying depth 'resolution' better than the physics fluctuation that constitutes the signal.

LuAG kinetics confound in the between-build comparison (CTR ratio ~ light ratio): keep the softened 'light yield governs' wording and lead with the MIXED in-event kill-shot, or a scintillator-timing referee will object.

experts.lens: HEP calorimetry state-of-the-art: precision-timing benchmarking (CMS BTL ~30-60 ps/track, CMS ECAL Phase-2 ~30 ps high-E, ATLAS HGTD ~35-50 ps, LHCb SPACAL W-GAGG R&D ~15-20 ps per shower, crystal bars 10-30 ps) and EM energy/position resolution norms (10%/sqrtE-class sampling calorimeters, ~1 mm crystal-ECAL localization, Awes log-weighting).
paper1_assessment: [+] The headline is genuinely competitive: 27 ps measured at 150 GeV with a 17.5 ps asymptote (Fig 27, Eq 2) sits at the leading edge of per-SHOWER timing — at or below LHCb SPACAL W-GAGG (~15-20 ps at >=80 GeV) and clearly better than CMS ECAL Phase-2 (~30 ps) and BTL (~30-60 ps, though BTL is per-MIP, a different regime) — and it comes from a 14x14 mm2 ultra-compact module, which is the differentiating selling point.

[+] The six-estimator comparison (Sec 6, Fig 27 Left) and the amplitude-binned analysis (Figs 21-26) are methodologically honest: they correctly identify detected light as the driver of sigma_t and show the full estimator family rather than only the best number — this anticipates the photostatistics story and is better practice than many timing test-beam papers.

[+] Sec 5.1.3 explicitly scopes the 52.04%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31% result as shower-max sampling only, NOT module energy resolution — exactly the disclaimer needed; the dual-gain deliberate-HG-saturation strategy (Sec 4, Fig 12) is a clever, documented design choice.

[-] Eq 2 carries no uncertainties, no chi2, no noise term, and b = 17.5 ps is an extrapolation beyond the highest beam energy fitted to six points with no error analysis or systematics budget anywhere in the timing section — by BTL-TDR/SPACAL standards this is the single largest gap; a timing referee cannot judge whether 17.5 vs 20 vs 25 ps is resolved.

[-] Neither term of Eq 2 is given a physical origin: 256/sqrtE is not decomposed into photostatistics vs threshold-slew vs shower fluctuations (no photoelectron budget despite the Fig 8 simulation being in npe/MeV), and the 17.5 ps floor is unexplained — the shower-depth-fluctuation identity (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 1 X0) that your own analysis later established is absent.

[-] The adopted BestMinus = (DW-UP)/2 cancels the shower arrival time itself; the paper never states that this is a self-referenced differential (physically a depth estimator), and the collider-relevant ABSOLUTE time-of-arrival resolution is only implicit in BestPlus-MCP with an asserted ~10 ps MCP subtraction — while the real reference chain (71-74 ps inter-group DRS4 domino jitter, measurable as MCP1-MCP2) is invisible. A reviewer from the MTD/HGTD community will not be able to extract the number they care about.

[-] The deliberate-saturation fixed-threshold method's time walk is never quantified, the 0.2 ns satellite (~15% of events, Figs 18-19) is attributed to a one-sample digitizer shift but neither corrected nor removed, and there is no DRS4 timing-calibration discussion (cell width, stop cell) — together these read as uncontrolled instrumental effects to a digitizer-literate referee.
paper2_timing: Absolute-vs-differential decomposition section (mandatory): write the two-end algebra explicitly (t_up/t_dw = t_0 -+ z/v + ref), report BOTH the depth-limited (DW-UP)/2 ladder AND the absolute shower time T_abs = mean(hg_led) vs the MCP with the full reference budget — 71-74 ps inter-group DRS4 jitter (mcp_jitter figure), ~15 ps after stop-cell correction, ~10-20 ps MCP intrinsic common-mode — and state plainly which number is the time-of-arrival resolution. Without this, the 17.5-19.5 ps floor will be attacked as 'not a clock measurement'.

Photoelectron budget to settle whether 256/sqrtE is understood: convert per-build LG charge to Npe (SiPM gain or relative LY), then test a vs 1/sqrt(Npe) (photostatistics) against 1/Npe (fixed-threshold slew). The current cross-build ratio is the tell: a_LuAG/a_DSB1 = 455/201 = 2.26 for a ~2.3-3x light ratio — between sqrt-LY (1.7) and linear-LY (3) scaling, i.e. a photostat+slew mixture. Pair with the measured slew table (ped RMS 1.31 mV; dV/dt 102->279 mV/ns => single-channel slew 22->12 ps at the foot) and a GEANT4 estimate of the shower-fluctuation share. This is what elevates the paper to BTL/SPACAL-TDR level of understanding.

Floor physics made quantitative, not asserted: overlay measured per-build b (DSB1 19.5+-1.1, LuAG 19.8+-5.6, TENERGY 21.6+-2.7) on the GEANT4 shower-max depth-fluctuation RMS converted via v_fiber = 200 mm/ns (~1 X0 = 5.4 mm -> ~27 ps two-end, ~ floor/sqrt-combinatorics), AND validate in-data that (DW-UP)/2 tracks depth: the MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 must shift ~+27 ps per e-folding of E (X0/v) across 25-150 GeV, matching <z_max> ~ X0*ln(E). This converts 'consistent with 17.5' into a mechanism and pre-empts 'is the plateau just systematics'.

Floor honesty package exactly as planned: per-technique floors with errors and the 4.2-sigma cfd05/lgcfd distinction; both 1/sqrtE and 1/E forms with chi2 (2.1 vs 12.1); PDG scaling and the a-b correlation (rho ~ -0.88); headline the MEASURED 25.3 ps @150 GeV (brightest-1000) AND the full-fiducial ~27-29 ps side by side; state that LuAG's brightest 150 GeV point is still ~42 ps and never reaches the extrapolated floor in data.

The MIXED kill-shot with full rigor: document the same-layer pairwise CFD-difference solve, quote ratio 0.99 (chi2/ndf 0.4/5) with errors, and add the scintillation-kinetics discussion a timing expert will demand — LuAG:Ce decay/rise kinetics vs DSB1 tau = 3.5 ns, why species is irrelevant at multi-hundred-pe threshold crossings (cite Gundacker/Lucchini on rise time vs CTR). Note the MIXED corner-material map is data-inferred — confirm against the logbook before this figure anchors the thesis.

Method-defense set: lgcfd HG/LG calibration stability (slope drift <=2% vs E), the in-data demonstration that lgcfd removes the published 0.2 ns satellite (a checkable claim — show the before/after distributions), the cfd05 time-walk-on-brightest figure (the documented motivation), the selection-systematics table (<=1-1.5 ps, floor survives), OOS matched-fraction folding, monotonic adopted-method ladders for all four builds, and the statement that published cfd05 numbers are reproduced within 1.6 ps (continuity with NIM A 1068).

State-of-the-art context table/figure (currently absent from the program's papers): RADiCAL measured points vs CMS BTL (per-MIP, flag the regime difference), CMS ECAL Phase-2, ATLAS HGTD, LHCb SPACAL W-GAGG/W-polystyrene per-shower numbers, LYSO crystal bars — with citations (BTL TDR; HGTD TDR; SPACAL beam tests e.g. Martinazzoli et al.; CMS ECAL timing test beams). Per-shower vs per-track must be labeled or the comparison gets rejected.

Wording discipline: 'shower-max two-end differential resolution' vs 'time-of-arrival resolution'; 'light yield governs' (not 'species does not matter' unqualified); floors are per-technique extrapolations; never headline a revision of 17.5 ps — the data CONFIRM it.
paper3_energy_position: Framing of 52%/sqrtE (decisive for acceptance): lead with what the shower-max energy estimator is FOR (timing correlation, position, module-ID in arrays), and add the GEANT4 sampled-fraction calculation — what percent of the shower the ~3 tiles/4 capillaries sample — plus a sim prediction reproducing 52%/sqrtE + 9.31% from sampling + photostatistics + lateral-leakage fluctuations. Position the 10%/sqrtE goal strictly as the Fig-28 enhanced-module design target supported by simulation; never put 52% and 10% on the same axis as comparable quantities.

Decompose Eq 1 term by term: noise term 31.62%/E validated against measured pedestal RMS summed over 8 LG channels; constant 9.31% budgeted into longitudinal-leakage droop (LG sum droops 9-20% by 150 GeV; show sigma_E/E before/after the Pb-glass correction that linearizes 9.2%->2.2%), transverse containment, inter-channel calibration (the Fig-14-style equalization), in-fiducial position dependence, and H2 beam momentum spread (~1%, subtract in quadrature, cite as literature value not measured).

Linearity systematic (mandatory): 29.4 mV/GeV with a residuals-vs-E panel in percent; E-type linearity (1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV slopes) as the first in-beam E-type characterization; explicit demonstration that the LG chain never clips (HG compresses by design).

Uniformity systematic (mandatory): transverse response maps vs wire-chamber (x,y) (transverseMaps exists), quantified response variation inside the fiducial (SE-U/SW-U run 10-15% low = longer upstream WLS path), and sigma_E/E vs fiducial radius — feed the measured non-uniformity into the constant-term budget.

Stability systematic (mandatory, currently the weakest flank): no temperature logging and a single 42.25 V bias means a referee will attack gain stability. Bound it in-data: run-to-run and spill-to-spill stability of the LG response (and MCP amplitude as a monitor) across each build's campaign, quoted as a percent and folded into the constant term; state the HDR2 gain temperature coefficient and that observed drifts bound the effect. Absence of monitoring must be addressed explicitly, not silently.

Position analysis to world-class: (a) reconcile the wire-chamber resolution numbers BEFORE publishing — a 1.5 mm residual is mathematically impossible against a 3.6 mm comparator (sigma_res^2 = sigma_cap^2 + sigma_WC^2), so either the effective WC resolution is ~1 mm (the kWC_resBin figure) and the 3.6 mm self-cal is inflated (beam divergence/method), or something is correlated; (b) add the log-weighted centroid (Awes et al., NIM A311) vs the linear/TLinearFitter estimator with S-curve bias correction; (c) resolution vs E for ALL energies (expect ~1/sqrtE-ish, ~1-1.5 mm @150 to ~3-4 mm @25-50 GeV) and vs impact position; (d) quote the capillary resolution as <=1.5 mm @150 GeV, tracker-comparator-limited, meeting the 'few mm' program goal — sub-mm claims only after the WC reconciliation. Context: CMS ECAL ~1 mm @ ~45 GeV with 22 mm crystals; shashlik experiments typically few mm — <=1.5 mm from a single 14 mm cell is a strong, defensible result.

Honor the binning guardrail (>=3.5-4 mm bins for any position-dependent analysis) and the alignment chain (module center (6.58,4.66), MCP-RADiCAL 0.79 mm) as a documented co-registration section — referees on the 4D claim will check that the three observables share one validated frame.

4D capstone upgrades: extend fourD_demo to show BOTH x and y residuals; add the observable correlation matrix (t, E, x, y) on the same TENERGY event sample to demonstrate simultaneity and (in)dependence; quote all-fiducial AND best-bin timing (57 / ~35 ps) without conflating them.

Selection systematics: containment-cut (30% Pb-glass) variation, fiducial-radius variation, and the 4-population event classification (good-EM/punch-through/halo/off-axis, 95.3% efficiency @150) as the cleanliness defense for both energy and position results.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: A demonstrated SIMULTANEOUS per-event 4-coordinate measurement at shower max in one ultra-compact module: transverse position x,y (<=1.5 mm at 150 GeV, tracker-comparator-limited; ~0.9 mm TENERGY residual), shower-max energy (sigma_E/E ~12-14% at 150 GeV, 52%/sqrtE form, WLS-species-independent 13.5-15%), and time (~25-35 ps best-selection, 27 ps published-style), each validated against external truth (wire chamber, beam energy, MCP) in one co-registered frame — i.e. '4D (x,y,E_showermax,t) reconstruction demonstrated in a single RADiCAL module'. The depth-fluctuation identity (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 1 X0) can be presented as evidence the module is SENSITIVE to the longitudinal coordinate.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: (1) '4D calorimeter meeting program goals' — the energy coordinate is shower-max sampling (52%/sqrtE), not the 10%/sqrtE full-module resolution, and the timing headline is a self-referenced differential whose absolute time-of-arrival counterpart is reference-limited (~50 ps with hg_led; 71-74 ps inter-group DRS4 budget). (2) 5D as a measurement — no event-by-event depth determination has been validated against an independent estimator; only the WIDTH of (DW-UP)/2 has been matched to the expected 1 X0 fluctuation, which is a consistency argument, not a per-event z measurement. (3) Anything about arrays/full showers — this is one module; the 3x3 is future work.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Two quick analyses on existing reduced data unlock an honest 5D claim: (1) event-by-event correlation of the two INDEPENDENT depth estimators — timing depth z_t = v_fiber*(DW-UP)/2 vs the DW/UP LG amplitude-asymmetry depth z_A (attenuation-based) — a significant correlation with the expected slope makes z a measured per-event coordinate with sigma_z ~ 4-5 mm (~1 X0); (2) the mean of (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) across 25-150 GeV compared to the GEANT4 <z_max>(E) — expect ~+27 ps (= X0/v_fiber) per e-folding of energy. If both pass, claim '5D: x, y, z(shower depth), E_sm, t'; if only (2) passes, claim demonstrated statistical depth sensitivity and keep 4D.

killer_additions: The 'depth dial' figure: mean (DW-UP)/2 and mean DW/UP amplitude asymmetry vs beam energy overlaid on GEANT4 <z_max>(E)/v_fiber, plus the in-event scatter of the two depth estimators. It converts the timing FLOOR (Paper 2's limitation) into a measured longitudinal COORDINATE (Paper 3's fifth dimension) — one figure that links both papers, explains the 17.5 ps floor mechanistically, and unlocks the 5D claim. No other compact-calorimeter R&D program has published this.

A first-principles closure of sigma_t(E): per-build measured photoelectron budget (LG charge -> Npe) feeding a predicted slew + photostat + depth-floor curve overlaid on the 4-build money plot — deriving, not just fitting, the 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps form. This is the difference between 'we measured a competitive number' and 'we understand the detector at TDR level', and it resolves the currently ambiguous a-scaling (455/201 = 2.26 vs light ratio ~2.3-3x).

A state-of-the-art landscape figure (sigma_t vs E): RADiCAL points/band alongside CMS BTL, CMS ECAL Phase-2, ATLAS HGTD, LHCb SPACAL, crystal bars, with per-shower vs per-MIP regimes labeled and detector volume annotated — the instantly quotable visual that makes '17-20 ps class timing from a 14x14x135 mm3 module' the memorable takeaway of the series.
referee_risks: Differential-vs-absolute timing attack: '(DW-UP)/2 cancels the shower arrival time; your clock-referenced resolution is 50-70 ps' — fatal if not pre-empted by the explicit two-end algebra, the reference-jitter budget (71-74 ps inter-group DRS4, stop-cell-corrected ~15 ps), and disciplined wording separating depth-differential from time-of-arrival.

Position inconsistency: a 1.5 mm capillary-vs-WC residual is impossible against a 3.6 mm WC resolution; the two memory-documented WC numbers (~1 mm effective vs 3.6 mm self-cal) must be reconciled before any deconvolved capillary resolution is quoted, or the position section collapses under one referee question.

Floor-as-extrapolation: b is quoted beyond the maximum beam energy, LuAG's best measured point (~42 ps) never approaches its extrapolated ~20 ps floor, and a/b are ~-0.88 anti-correlated — must present per-form (1/sqrtE vs 1/E) and per-technique floors with errors and headline only measured values.

MIXED kill-shot foundation: the corner material map (NE+SW DSB1, NW+SE LuAG) is data-inferred, flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook, and the LuAG NW capillary type in the LUAG build is unconfirmed — the thesis-bearing figure rests on these; confirm or disclose the inference with the brightness-tag evidence.

LuAG kinetics confound: LuAG:Ce CTR ratio (~2.6x LYSO) approximately equals the light ratio (~2.3x), so cross-build ordering alone cannot separate light from kinetics — only the in-event MIXED ratio 0.99 defends the softened 'light yield governs' wording; any harder claim will be attacked by scintillator-timing experts.

Deliberate-saturation method: fixed-threshold LED on a clipped pulse implies amplitude-dependent time walk and the published 0.2 ns one-sample satellite (~15% of events); Paper 2 must quantify the walk and DEMONSTRATE (not assert) that lgcfd removes the satellite, or the method section inherits the parent paper's weakness.

No temperature/bias monitoring: single 42.25 V working point with no logged temperature invites a gain-stability attack on the 9.31% constant term and on cross-build comparability — must be bounded in-data via run/spill response stability before submission.

Energy-resolution framing trap: any presentation that lets 52%/sqrtE be read as 'the calorimeter's resolution' (or compared on-axis with the 10%/sqrtE goal) triggers a 'this is not competitive calorimetry' rejection; the sampled-fraction simulation and shower-max-estimator framing are the shield.

Selection-bias optics: brightest-1000 headlines select the best-measured subset; report full-fiducial numbers alongside, show the OOS matched-fraction folding and the systematics sweep, and keep the published 27.4 ps cfd05 best-bin as the standing OOS prior to defend continuity with NIM A 1068.

experts.lens: Scientific-figures expert: information density, comparison topology (overlay vs grid vs ratio), money-plot placement, appendix discipline, and production quality for NIM A.
paper1_assessment: [+] Fig 27 is an exemplary closing pair: the six-method overlay (left) makes the estimator choice visually self-evident, and the BestMinus-with-fit panel (right) is a genuine money plot — Eq. 2 (256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps) reads directly off it. This two-panel 'survey then verdict' topology is the strongest figure decision in the paper and should be the template for Papers 2/3.

[+] The evidence chain is fully auditable: Fig 12 (average waveforms, dual-gain) -> Figs 14-16 (amplitude distributions) -> Figs 18-20 (timing distributions at three aggregation levels) -> Figs 21-26 (amplitude-binned sigma_t). A referee can trace every headline number back to raw pulse shapes — rare and valuable. Figs 7-9 (GEANT4 depth profile, sigma_t vs light yield, transverse compactness) motivate the shower-max concept before any data appear.

[-] Figs 21-26 are the cardinal sin: SIX nearly identical 19-sub-panel per-energy grids (~114 sub-histograms total) delivering one sentence of physics ('sigma_t falls with measured amplitude; bins 6-8 define BestMinus'). The correct topology is ONE overlay — sigma_t vs amplitude with six energy series on shared axes (exactly what the session's pub_res figure now does, showing the curves collapse onto one sigma(light) law) — with at most one representative bin-grid in an appendix. The published set spends ~25% of its page count on its least dense figures, and the amplitude-collapse physics is never actually shown as a single comparative figure.

[-] Redundancy and omission coexist: Figs 18/19/20 show timing distributions three overlapping ways, and Fig 13 spends 16 panels on a qualitative beam-map point — yet there is NO zoomed figure of the 0.2 ns satellite (claimed '~15% area, little effect' but never quantified), no systematics/selection-stability figure, no floor-physics figure (17.5 ps exists only as a fit parameter, never connected to the ~1 X0 shower-depth fluctuation it represents), and no fit-form comparison. Production quality is also uneven: matplotlib-default styling, unitless legend sigmas ('sigma = 62'), inconsistent fonts between figure families, and two full pages of photographs (Figs 10-11).
paper2_timing: MONEY PLOT (Sec. results, referenced from abstract): the four-build sigma_t(E) overlay (light_yield_thesis) — brightest-1000 (DW-UP)/2, adopted source per build (lgcfd for DSB1/MIXED, led for LuAG/TENERGY), a/sqrtE (+) b fits with an inset parameter table (DSB1 a=201/b=20+-1; TENERGY 240/22+-3; MIXED 233/35+-2; LuAG 455/20+-6). MANDATORY: restore the regressed error bars (the published paper had them — a referee will notice), annotate that LuAG/MIXED/TENERGY fits span 50-150 GeV only, and state the matched selection (same K=1000 at every E) in the caption.

KILL-SHOT as the structural climax (own subsection, after the money plot): mixed_h2h — in-event per-capillary intrinsic sigma_t vs E, DSB1 corners (red) vs LuAG:Ce corners (green) inside ONE MIXED module, with a LOWER RATIO PANEL pinned at 0.99 (chi2/ndf 0.4/5). The ratio-panel topology is essential: it converts 'two overlapping curves' into a quantified null at a glance. This is the confound-free defense of the softened thesis ('light yield governs') against the LuAG-kinetics objection (LuAG CTR ~2.6x ~ light ratio 2.3x).

Method figure with difference/ratio sub-panel: cfd05 vs lgcfd on IDENTICAL DSB1 brightest-1000 events (method_compare), plus a lower panel of delta-sigma(E) showing the energy-dependent gain (~6 ps @25, ~0 @50-75, ~3 ps @100-150) — proves the gain lives in the noise-foot and saturation regimes, not the stochastic term (a 205->201). Pair with ONE mechanism waveform (pulse.png): single clipped 820 mV event, cfd05 stuck on the 102 mV/ns foot vs lgcfd on the 381 mV/ns recovered edge, slopes annotated.

Replace the published Figs 21-26 pattern with the collapse figure (pub_res): sigma_t vs shower amplitude, one series per beam energy, all six collapsing onto one sigma(light) curve with the floor asymptote dashed — this is the published paper's 114 sub-panels compressed into one panel that says MORE. Relegate per-energy (DW-UP)/2 distribution grids (timing_allmethods / method_dist) to an appendix; keep ONE main-text distribution figure: DSB1 150 GeV (DW-UP)/2 with the Gaussian-core fit, the 0.2 ns satellite explicitly zoomed/shaded, and the cfd05-vs-lgcfd satellite removal shown as a measured delta (checkable claim, currently only asserted).

Floor-honesty figure (new, required by the panel's FATAL list): per-build dual-form fit comparison — photostat 1/sqrtE (chi2/ndf ~2) vs slew 1/E (~12) with both b values quoted (DSB1 19.5 vs 26.1; LuAG 19.8 vs 39.3), plus the pub_thresh cumulative-threshold plateau showing the floor is a data plateau, not a fit artifact. Caption states plainly: LuAG's brightest measured point is 42 ps — 'material-independent ~20 ps floor' is a geometry-predicted extrapolation (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t -> ~1 X0 = 5.4 mm), headline the MEASURED 25.3 ps @150.

Eight-source method survey as a TABLE (a, b +- err, chi2/ndf per source per build), not 32 curves — one supporting 2x2 figure (timing_allmethods) in the appendix. The optimization appendix (optimization.png: adopted recovered edge below the entire clipped-CFD family for every build; K~1000 knee; fiducial plateau r=3.0; HG-threshold flat) plus the systematics figure+table (total selection syst <=1-1.5 ps; floor survives) are what let two different adopted sources per build survive review — without them 'lgcfd for bright, led for dim' reads as cherry-picking.

Appendix validations: OOS matched-fraction fold stability (foldA ~ foldB ~ full); the MCP1-MCP2 71 ps inter-group jitter figure with the one-sentence explanation of why same-group (DW-UP)/2 sits below it (deliberate channel-map design — this preempts the 'how is 25 ps below your 71 ps reference' referee question); the 32-channel HG=a+b*LG calibration grid (REBUILD hglg — currently garbled axes, the only unpublishable figure) with one representative channel promoted to main text.

Wording/citations: 'light yield GOVERNS timing' (never 'not species'); CONFIRM 17.5 ps, never revise; cite C. Hu et al. NIM A 954 (2020) for LuAG:Ce, the parent NIM A 1068 Eq. 2 as the baseline, CMS MTD (~30 ps) and HGCAL for FCC context (PU~1000), and S. Ritt's DRS4 papers for the domino-phase jitter. State explicitly this delivers the parent paper's Sec. 7.2 promise in 25 GeV steps.
paper3_energy_position: Energy backbone as ONE two-panel overlay (config_resolution): (a) LG linearity, all builds, mV/GeV slopes annotated; (b) sigma_E/E(E) with a/sqrtE (+) b/E (+) c fits, all four builds on shared axes converging to ~14% @150 (13.5-15% spread). Caption must repeat the parent paper's discipline verbatim: this is SHOWER-MAX SAMPLING (~3 LYSO tiles ~ 1 R_M), not full-module resolution — the 9.31%-class constant term is restricted transverse sampling, not a failure against the 0.7% program goal. Add a small leakage panel: Pb-glass linearization of the LG droop (9.2% -> 2.2%).

Position result with the comparator made explicit: main figure = capillary estimator vs wire-chamber truth (2D COLZ + ProfileX, x and y) PLUS a residual distribution panel with the WC's own ~3.6 mm resolution drawn as a shaded band. The 1.5 mm residual MUST be captioned as tracker-comparator-limited, never as a calibrated capillary resolution (residual < comparator resolution is a contradiction a referee will seize). Include the wireChamberResolution measurement figure in the same paper — the comparator must be characterized where it is used. Respect the >=3.5-4 mm binning guardrail in any position-binned analysis.
