Tracker-free internal position cross-check (new analysis, the release valve for the comparator ceiling): build two independent corner-light estimators from disjoint information (e.g. up-end-only vs down-end-only light fractions, or diagonal corner pairs), take their per-event difference /sqrt2 — an internal sigma_x that needs no wire chamber. This converts 'residual is comparator-limited' from an apology into a measurement.

E-type characterization (the in-beam analog of published Fig 4 — frame it exactly that way): two-panel etype_vs_ttype (LG slope 1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV, linear -> a uniform energy element; single-channel timing comparable to T-type) plus per-capillary sigma_E/E(E). One figure, one table row. TENERGY is the only E-type in-beam data in existence — say so.

4D capstone (closing figure, graphical-abstract candidate): the TENERGY 150 GeV three-panel — TIME (DW-UP)/2 over 3 T-types, sigma_t = 57 ps all-fiducial; ENERGY 8-LG sum, sigma_E/E = 11.9%; POSITION 4-corner fit minus WC, sigma_x = 0.9 mm — explicitly from the SAME event sample, one module. Quote consistently: 57 ps all-fiducial is the headline, 35 ps best-bin secondary; never lead with the better number. Co-registration inputs (module center (6.58,4.66), MCP alignment 0.79 mm, WC 3.6 mm) go in an appendix table.

Depth/5th-dimension figure (the bridge to Paper 2): mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) over 25-150 GeV (DSB1, six points), converted to mm via v_fiber = 200 mm/ns, overlaid on the GEANT4 shower-max depth progression (the parent paper's Fig 7 already shows max moving from layers 8-10 to 11-13) — and the floor identity sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-5 mm ~ 1 X0. One observable read two ways: Paper 2's floor is Paper 3's depth signal.

Supporting/appendix: ONE transverse response-flatness map in main text (HG/LG ratio flat in fiducial; SE-U/SW-U ~10-15% low from WLS path length), full 8-capillary map grids to appendix — do not repeat the published Fig 13's 16-panel sprawl; containment classification (Pop A-D, 95.3% efficiency @150, the 30% Pb-glass cut) as one appendix figure; beam-spot 2.9 mm and the ~1% H2 momentum-spread caveat stated in the event-selection section.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: An honest 4D DEMONSTRATION at shower max: one TENERGY module simultaneously measures, per shower from one event sample at 150 GeV, time (57 ps all-fiducial / ~35 ps best-bin, MCP-free), shower-max energy (sigma_E/E ~ 11.9-14%), and transverse position (0.9-1.5 mm residual against a ~3.6 mm tracker). The phrase to use is '4D (x, y, E, t) measurement capability demonstrated in a single ultra-compact module at shower max' — backed by the three-panel same-events capstone figure. Depth sensitivity (the 5th coordinate) can be claimed as PHYSICS: the (DW-UP)/2 floor is quantitatively the ~1 X0 shower-max depth fluctuation (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-5 mm ~ X0 = 5.4 mm).
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: (1) 4D at collider-spec performance — energy is shower-max sampling (52%/sqrtE-class), nowhere near the 10%/sqrtE (+) 0.7% goal, and must never be presented as module energy resolution; (2) a calibrated mm-level position RESOLUTION — the 1.5 mm (and 0.9 mm) numbers are residuals against a comparator with worse resolution than the result; (3) 5D as a per-event depth MEASUREMENT — (DW-UP)/2 has no independent depth truth in this dataset (no longitudinally segmented reference, no per-event simulation matching), so 'depth-sensitive' yes, 'depth-measuring per event' no; (4) any of it beyond 150 GeV / single-module / electron-beam conditions.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Three cheap analyses on existing reduced data: (a) mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) across 25-150 GeV overlaid on the GEANT4 shower-max progression via v_fiber=200 mm/ns — if the slope matches ~X0 per e-fold, RADiCAL measurably TRACKS shower depth at the population level, upgrading the claim to '4D + demonstrated longitudinal depth sensitivity' (honest 5D-capability language); (b) per-event correlation of (DW-UP)/2 with shower-max LG sum at fixed beam energy (deeper showers sample differently) — per-event depth physics without external truth; (c) the tracker-free split-estimator position self-consistency, giving an internal sigma_x that is not comparator-capped. All three use branches already in the rad tree.

killer_additions: THE UNIVERSAL COLLAPSE FIGURE (Paper 2 graphical abstract): sigma_t vs detected light (sum_lg) with ALL four builds and ALL energies on a single panel, collapsing onto one sigma(light) law with the ~20 ps floor as the dashed asymptote, and the MIXED in-event ratio (0.99) as an inset. The entire thesis — light yield governs, species irrelevant, floor is geometry — in one panel. It is the exact inverse of the published Figs 21-26: maximum physics per square centimeter, and no other timing-calorimetry paper has an in-module two-species control sample to put on it.

THE 5TH-COORDINATE FIGURE (bridges Papers 2 and 3, likely the most-cited figure of the pair): mean (DW-UP)/2 converted to mm (v_fiber = 200 mm/ns) vs ln(E), overlaid on GEANT4 shower-max depth — the timing FLOOR of Paper 2 re-read as the DEPTH SIGNAL of Paper 3. It transforms a limitation ('we plateau at 20 ps') into a capability ('the plateau IS a depth measurement of ~1 X0 precision') and gives the program its 5D narrative with data it already has.

THE 4D SHOWER PASSPORT (Paper 3 closing/graphical abstract): one real 150 GeV TENERGY event rendered as a single composite — the 8 raw waveforms, the extracted t (DW-UP)/2, the LG-sum E, the 4-corner light asymmetry with the reconstructed (x,y) on the module footprint, and the DW-UP depth arrow — 'one shower, one module, four coordinates'. Event-display-style figures are what make instrumentation papers memorable and what review talks lift verbatim.
referee_risks: Position paradox: a 1.5 mm (or 0.9 mm) residual against a tracker measured at ~3.6 mm resolution is internally inconsistent if presented as a resolution — either the WC number or the framing must give. Present strictly as residual + comparator-limited, show the WC characterization in-paper, and ideally add the tracker-free self-consistency estimate.

Floor material-independence is an extrapolation: LuAG's best measured point is 42 ps and never reaches the fitted ~20 ps floor; LuAG's b carries +-5.6 ps. Must show both fit forms with chi2 and headline measured sigma@150, or a referee will call the shared-floor claim fit-driven.

Two different adopted timing sources per build (lgcfd vs led) invites an apples-to-oranges attack — only survivable with the optimization appendix showing the adopted edge beats the entire CFD family per build, plus the systematics sweep (and cfd05-vs-lgcfd correctly EXCLUDED from systematics as the thesis, with that exclusion justified in print).

MIXED corner material map is data-inferred (inferMixed.C), flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook, with NW the weakest match — the kill-shot figure rests on it. Confirm against the logbook before submission; same for the LuAG-build NW capillary type (T vs E, 'Added full LuAG Cap' logbook line).
Error bars regressed off the money plot (the published paper HAD them — Fig 17/27); flagged FATAL by the expert panel. Also rebuild hglg (garbled axes).

Satellite-removal claim (lgcfd kills the 0.2 ns satellite) is currently asserted, not demonstrated — the published paper documented the satellite (Figs 18-19), so reviewers of the follow-up will expect the measured before/after.
Energy-range asymmetry: only DSB1 has 25 GeV; fits for the other three builds span 50-150. Captions and the fit table must state ranges or the cross-build a-parameters are not comparable.

4D demo scope: single energy (150 GeV), single module, electron beam, and a quoted sigma_t that varies by selection (57 vs 35 ps) — quote the all-fiducial number first, always, and bound the claim to 'demonstration'.
Shower-max energy resolution will be misread as module resolution unless every figure caption repeats the restriction (the parent paper's Sec 5.1.3 wording is the template — reuse it).
Beam momentum spread (~1%) is a literature value for H2, not measured per-run — flag it where energy resolution is fit, as the parent paper did ('understood to be <2%').

experts.lens: Future-collider requirements mapping: FCC-hh forward EM calorimetry (radiation + pileup timing at <mu>~1000), 10 TeV muon-collider BIB suppression (30-60 ps cell-level windows), FCC-ee luminosity/MDI calorimetry, and fixed-target/forward space-constrained regions — where RADiCAL's measured 2023 performance actually lands against published requirement numbers, and what radiation-hardness language the dataset can honestly support.
paper1_assessment: [+] Collider framing is structurally right: keywords (FCC, ILC, 10 TeV pCM, Fast-timing), Section 1 anchors to DOE PRD/BRN, ECFA DRD6 and CPAD RDC9 [refs 6-11], and refs [1-5] cite the FCC CDRs including Aleksa et al. arXiv:1912.09962 'Calorimeters for the FCC-hh'. The MCP-free (DW-UP)/2 BestMinus estimator (Fig. 27, Eq. 2: 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps, 27 ps at 150 GeV) is the collider-transferable result — a real experiment has no MCP, and self-referenced differential timing that cancels reference/timebase jitter is exactly what a 4D calorimeter cell must do.

[+] Honest scoping of the energy claim: Section 5.1.3 explicitly states sigma_E/E = 52.04%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31% is shower-max-restricted sampling (~3 LYSO tiles), NOT full-module resolution, protecting the 10%/sqrtE program goal from overclaim. The shower-max module-ID argument (Fig. 9: r<5 mm energy core inside a 14x14 mm module) is the genuinely unique forward-region selling point and is correctly identified as 'potentially powerful locational tool' for high-eta overlapping-shower regions.

[-] The Conclusions claim — 'potential to meet and exceed the timing requirements for FCC-ee and FCC-hh applications' — is never quantified anywhere in the paper. No requirement table exists: FCC-hh pileup mitigation at <mu>~1000 targets 5-10 ps (aggressive) to ~25-30 ps (4D object level) per the CDR/Aleksa; the measured 27 ps at 150 GeV with a 17.5 ps floor meets the 4D-object tier but does not 'exceed' the 5-10 ps tier. The muon collider — arguably RADiCAL's best fit, since 27 ps sits comfortably inside the 30-60 ps cell-level BIB-suppression windows used in IMCC detector studies — is absent entirely despite the '10 TeV pCM' keyword. A collider reviewer cannot check the headline claim against anything.

[-] Radiation hardness is asserted by association, not mapped: the abstract promises 'high irradiation (FCC-ee, FCC-hh ... forward physics environments)' and refs [14-16] are cited once in Section 1, but the build actually measured uses DSB1 ORGANIC WLS — the non-rad-hard reference material — and the paper never states what dose/fluence (FCC-hh forward EM reaches O(MGy-GGy) and up to ~10^17-10^18 n_eq/cm^2) any tested component tolerates. The rad-hard LuAG:Ce variant appears only as Section 7 future work (ref [24], Hu et al. NIM A 954). The gap between 'measured 27 ps' and 'survives the forward region' is left entirely to the reader; no sigma_t vs impact-position study (Fig. 13 shows the position dependence exists) and no benchmark against the closest deployed analog, LHCb Upgrade-II SpaCal (W/GAGG, ~1 MGy / 6x10^15 n_eq/cm^2 design point, ~15-30 ps in test beam).
paper2_timing: REQUIREMENTS TABLE (the single most important addition; memory already flags 'FCC quantitative context' as an expert-panel fatal fix): one table with rows = (a) FCC-hh forward EM: <mu>~1000, 5-10 ps aspirational / ~30 ps 4D-object tier, dose up to O(GGy) and ~10^17-18 n_eq/cm^2 at |eta|>4 [FCC-hh CDR EPJ ST 228 (2019) 755; Aleksa et al. arXiv:1912.09962]; (b) 10 TeV muon collider ECAL: 30-60 ps cell-level timing inside ~+-250 ps BIB acceptance windows, ~kGy/yr + ~10^14-15 n_eq/cm^2/yr [IMCC interim report arXiv:2407.12450; arXiv:2203.07964; CRILIN as benchmark]; (c) FCC-ee LumiCal/MDI: modest radiation, compactness premium, timing for Bhabha pile-up at Z-pole 20-25 ns bunch spacing; (d) deployed benchmarks: CMS MTD BTL ~30-60 ps, HGCAL ~25 ps high-E showers, LHCb SpaCal W/GAGG. Columns = sigma_t required, radiation environment, RADiCAL measured per build (DSB1 25.3 ps lgcfd / LuAG 41.9 ps led / MIXED / TENERGY at 150 GeV, brightest-1000), and the gap/path. This converts the unquantified published conclusion into a checkable claim.

REPOSITION LuAG AS THE COLLIDER RESULT, not the loser: the radiation-hard configuration (LuAG:Ce ceramic WLS + quartz capillary + LYSO:Ce + W — every optical component with demonstrated tolerance, cite refs [14-16] and Hu et al. NIM A 954 (2020) 161723) measures 42 ps at 150 GeV (a=455, b=19.8+-5.6), and the MIXED in-event kill-shot (ratio 0.99) proves the deficit vs DSB1 is pure photostatistics, not species/kinetics. Add an explicit engineering projection: recovering the x2.3 light deficit (published Section 7.3 already plans x2 LYSO thickness at shower max; plus coupling/PDE) takes a to ~300 and sigma_t(150) to ~31 ps, inside the muon-collider BIB band — without giving up radiation hardness. That is the sentence DRD6 reviewers will quote.

RADIATION-CLAIM DISCIPLINE PARAGRAPH: state plainly that (i) no irradiated module has been beam-tested and this dataset contains zero irradiation data; (ii) component-level tolerance rests on the companion papers — Anderson et al. arXiv:2203.12806 [14], Anderson et al. Instruments 6(3) 2022 [15], Wetzel et al. IEEE TNS 70 (2023) 1296 [16], Hu et al. [24]; (iii) the tested-here contribution is that the rad-hard WLS configuration is TIMING-VIABLE at equal conditions. Add an honest SiPM paragraph: HDR2 SiPMs at the module ends face the same forward fluence (10^14+ n_eq/cm^2 degrades dark counts/jitter); name mitigations (cooling, annealing, shielding/placement) or scope the claim to environments <=10^14.

ENERGY-REGIME ARGUMENT: forward-region EM objects at FCC-hh/fixed-target are 100 GeV-TeV, where sigma_t(E) is floor-dominated — so the operative collider numbers are the MEASURED 25.3-27 ps at 150 GeV and the per-technique floor b~20 ps (per floor-honesty rules: form-dependent extrapolation, both 1/sqrtE and 1/E quoted, LuAG never measured below 42 ps). State explicitly which requirement tier this meets (muon-collider BIB: yes, with margin; FCC-hh 4D-object ~30 ps: yes; FCC-hh aggressive 5-10 ps vertex-slicing: no, and say so) — preempting the referee who re-reads the published 'meet and exceed' line.

MCP-FREE / CLOCK-DISTRIBUTION FRAMING: the (DW-UP)/2 estimator cancels the measured 71-74 ps inter-DRS4-group domino jitter exactly (dataset_2023.md) — present this as a stand-in for real-experiment clock-distribution jitter, which is itself a ~10 ps problem at FCC scale. Differential self-timing that needs no external reference is a system-level advantage no crystal-bar competitor (BTL, CRILIN single-ended) shares; one paragraph plus the mcp_jitter figure.

BENCHMARK CITATIONS to add: LHCb Upgrade-II SpaCal test-beam results (the closest W/garnet shashlik-style rad-hard fast ECAL, LHCb-TDR-023 + SpaCal NIM papers), CRILIN (muon collider, PbF2/PWO-UF, ~20-40 ps targets), CMS MTD TDR (LYSO:Ce at 30-60 ps MIP-level — note RADiCAL times SHOWERS, a different and easier object, so the comparison must be framed per-object), CMS HGCAL timing. Without these the paper floats in a vacuum and a NIM A referee from any of those efforts will supply them less charitably.
paper3_energy_position: POSITION/ENERGY REQUIREMENTS TABLE mirroring Paper 2's: (a) FCC-ee LumiCal — 10^-4 absolute luminosity needs acceptance-edge definition; compact R_M and ~1 mm shower position in a tight MDI radial envelope is where the 14 mm module is uniquely suited (cite FCC-ee CDR EPJ ST 228 (2019) 261); (b) FCC-hh forward — two-shower separation and module-ID at |eta|>4 where track-matching fails; (c) muon collider — shower-pointing + position for BIB rejection; (d) fixed-target/forward spectrometers — the literal space-constrained slot. Columns: position/energy required, radiation, RADiCAL measured (<=1.5 mm residual, sigma_E/E 14% shower-max), path to goal (10%/sqrtE via Section 7.3 enhanced design + 3x3 array).

GUARDRAILED POSITION CLAIM (memory already mandates this): present 1.5 mm (and the 4D demo's 0.9 mm) as TRACKER-COMPARATOR-LIMITED upper limits — the wire chamber's own resolution is ~3.6 mm (wireChamberResolution.C) — i.e. 'consistent with the few-mm program goal and sufficient for module-ID'; never as a calibrated capillary resolution. Use >=3.5-4 mm bins for any position-dependent analysis per the binning guardrail.

MODULE-ID DEMONSTRATION WITH DATA: the published Fig. 9 pattern-recognition argument is simulation-only. Use WC-tagged off-center events to measure 4-corner energy-sharing vs impact position and quantify a 'which-module-was-hit' assignment efficiency across the 14x14 mm footprint — converting the unique forward-region selling point from a GEANT4 claim into a beam-validated one. This is the data analog of published Section 7.1 and the figure collider concept groups will reuse.

E-TYPE AS THE Fig.-28 ENABLER: characterize the TENERGY E-type as the energy element of the enhanced 18x18 module — measured 1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV (4-5x dimmer) but linear 50-150 GeV with flat constant-term-dominated per-cap resolution; state explicitly it is excluded from corner timing. Frame as the in-beam validation of published Fig. 4 (bench LED scan) and the prerequisite for the Section 7.3/7.4 design — reviewers tracking the published roadmap will look for exactly this closure.

ENERGY-CLAIM HYGIENE: keep the 52%/sqrtE + 9.3% / ~14% at 150 GeV result rigorously labeled as localized shower-max sampling (13.5-15% WLS-species-independent across builds is itself a useful result — energy at shower max does not care which rad-hard WLS you choose); add one GEANT4-backed projection paragraph for the full-module/3x3 path to 10%/sqrtE + 0.7%, citing the published Section 7.3 thickness argument, so the collider mapping table has a defensible 'path' column rather than an implied measured value.

RADIATION COLUMN FOR PAPER 3 TOO: position and energy at shower max are read on the same LG chain; note component tolerances by citation (refs 14-16, 24) and that FCC-ee LumiCal radiation levels (orders below FCC-hh forward) are already compatible with even the DSB1 organic configuration — the one environment where the measured-here build could be deployed as-is. That is an honest, attackproof deployment claim.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: 4D, honestly and now: the TENERGY capstone measures time (sigma_t ~57 ps all-fiducial, ~35 ps best-bin via MCP-free (DW-UP)/2 over 3 T-types), energy (sigma_E/E ~11.9% shower-max), and transverse position (<=0.9 mm residual, comparator-limited) SIMULTANEOUSLY from the same 150 GeV events in one 14x14x135 mm module — a genuine single-device (x,y,E,t) demonstration at shower max. Additionally claimable: the (DW-UP)/2 spread is quantitatively consistent with ~1 X0 shower-depth fluctuation (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-5 mm vs X0 = 5.4 mm), i.e. the device is depth-fluctuation-limited, which is itself evidence the longitudinal observable carries shower physics.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: 5D as a measured depth RESOLUTION: no independent depth truth exists in the dataset, so 'sigma_z ~ 5 mm' is currently an interpretation of a timing width, not a validated measurement; the DW/UP amplitude asymmetry route is uncalibrated (no attenuation-length scan). Also not claimable: 'validated 4D calorimeter technology for colliders' — one module, electrons only, 25-150 GeV, no array, no irradiated-module beam test, position comparator-limited at 3.6 mm, and shower-max energy only. And do not let the 4D demo's 57 ps be quoted as 'the' timing — it is the 3-capillary TENERGY number, not the 4-capillary DSB1 25.3 ps.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Two pure-analysis steps on existing reduced data upgrade 4D to an honest depth-sensitive '4D+depth' (5D-capable) claim: (1) plot the MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 vs beam energy across 25-150 GeV for all builds — shower max migrates ~X0*ln(E2/E1) ~ 9.7 mm from 25 to 150 GeV, predicting a ~48 ps centroid shift at v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns; agreement with GEANT4 t_max(E) (the published Fig. 7 simulation already shows the migration) demonstrates the depth axis RESPONDS to a known depth change; (2) in-event cross-correlation of the two independent depth proxies — (DW-UP)/2 time vs ln(A_DW/A_UP) amplitude asymmetry per event — any significant correlation proves per-event depth information above noise. Both use branches already in the rad tree; neither needs new beam time.

killer_additions: THE DEPTH-METER FIGURE (Paper 3, possibly previewed in Paper 2's discussion): mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) for all four builds overlaid on the GEANT4 shower-max migration (published Fig. 7 reused), plus the in-event time-vs-amplitude-asymmetry correlation. If the predicted ~48 ps centroid shift from 25 to 150 GeV appears, RADiCAL becomes the first compact module to MEASURE EM shower depth event-by-event via dual-end timing — a claim neither LHCb SpaCal nor CRILIN nor CMS MTD can make, and the legitimate basis for the 5D language.

REQUIREMENTS-OVERLAY MONEY PLOT (Paper 2): take light_yield_thesis.png and draw the requirement bands directly on it as horizontal zones — muon-collider BIB 30-60 ps, FCC-hh 4D-object ~30 ps, FCC-hh aggressive 5-10 ps — with all four builds' measured points and the LuAG light-recovery arrow (x2 light -> ~31 ps). One figure that answers 'so what?' for every collider audience and gets lifted into DRD6/CPAD review documents verbatim.

THE 'RAD-HARD CONFIGURATION IS DEPLOYABLE' SYNTHESIS BOX (both papers): a half-page table crossing environment (FCC-ee LumiCal / muon-collider ECAL / FCC-hh forward) x (dose, fluence, sigma_t needed, sigma_E needed, space envelope) x (which RADiCAL build configuration qualifies TODAY by measurement + component citation, which needs the Section 7.3 light upgrade, which needs SiPM relocation/replacement R&D). The honesty gradient itself — 'as-is / one engineering step / open R&D' — is what makes it credible and memorable versus the published paper's single unquantified sentence.
referee_risks: Repeating the published 'meet and exceed the timing requirements for FCC-ee and FCC-hh' without numbers: FCC-hh pileup vertex-slicing at <mu>~1000 nominally wants 5-10 ps; measured 25-27 ps meets the ~30 ps 4D-object tier only. Any reviewer from the FCC-hh calorimeter group (Aleksa et al. is cited!) will check. Tier the claim explicitly.

Radiation-hardness overreach: the dataset contains ZERO irradiation data and the best-timing build uses organic DSB1. All hardness statements must be component-level citations (refs [14] arXiv:2203.12806, [15] Instruments 6(3) 2022, [16] IEEE TNS 70 (2023) 1296, [24] Hu NIM A 954) plus the measured viability of the LuAG configuration — never 'the module is radiation hard as demonstrated here'. Also acknowledge SiPM neutron vulnerability at 10^14+ n_eq/cm^2; silence on the photosensor is the first question from any HL-LHC-experienced referee.

LuAG floor b = 19.8 +- 5.6 ps is an extrapolation — measured LuAG never goes below ~42 ps. Claiming the rad-hard configuration 'reaches a ~20 ps floor' as if measured will be attacked; follow the locked floor-honesty rule (headline the measured sigma at 150, quote per-technique form-dependent floors).

Position numbers (1.5 mm, 0.9 mm) sit BELOW the wire chamber's own ~3.6 mm resolution; presenting them as capillary resolution rather than comparator-limited upper bounds is an immediate objection (guardrail already in paper_energy_position.md — enforce it in every collider-mapping sentence).

Object-type mismatch in benchmarks: CMS MTD's 30-60 ps is per-MIP, RADiCAL's 25 ps is per-150-GeV-shower, muon-collider BIB suppression involves soft out-of-time photons/neutrons hitting individual cells. Comparing across object types without saying so invites a 'apples-to-oranges' report. State per-object, per-energy conditions in every table row; data are electrons 25-150 GeV only — no MIP timing, no hadrons, no array.

The MIXED kill-shot (ratio 0.99) underpins the rad-hard-path argument, but the MIXED corner material map is data-inferred and flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook (dataset_2023.md VERIFY). Close that verification before submission — 'how do you know which corners are LuAG' is a one-line referee question with a currently soft answer.

Energy-resolution conflation: any table putting 10%/sqrtE in a 'RADiCAL' column without labeling it a design goal (measured: 52%/sqrtE + 9.3% shower-max sampling) will draw the same scrutiny the published paper carefully preempted in Section 5.1.3 — keep its discipline.

experts.lens: References & literature positioning — what Papers 2 and 3 must cite, benchmark against, and how to claim novelty credibly (competing fast-timing calorimetry, WLS/shashlik heritage, timing-method canon, 4D/5D framing)
paper1_assessment: [+] Program positioning is strong: the Introduction wires the work into the official planning canon (FCC CDRs refs 1–4, Aleksa FCC-hh calorimetry ref 5, DOE BRN ref 6, ECFA DRD6 ref 7, CPAD/RDC9 refs 8–10), and Eq. 2 (sigma_t = 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps) plus Fig. 27 give a clean, quotable baseline that follow-up papers can cite as the prior — this is the right skeleton to inherit.

[+] The six-estimator taxonomy (Sec. 6, Figs. 18–27: Downstream/Upstream/BestPlus/BestPlus-MCP/(DW-UP)/2/BestMinus) and the honest MCP-included vs MCP-free separation are self-contained and reproducible, and the explicit scoping of Eq. 1 as shower-max-only sampling resolution ('does not represent... full module', Sec. 5.1.3) pre-empts the worst misreading. Ref 22 (Cates et al., PMB 2018, SiPM front-end timing) shows the seed of methods awareness.

[-] FATAL GAP — no competitive context anywhere: 27 ps @ 150 GeV floats with no benchmark. Missing entirely: CMS MTD/BTL (the SAME LYSO:Ce + SiPM combination, TDR CERN-LHCC-2019-003, 30–60 ps/MIP), LHCb PicoCal/SPACAL W-GAGG beam tests (~15–20 ps EM-shower timing at comparable energies — the closest and arguably leading competitor; LHCb Upgrade II FTDR CERN-LHCC-2021-012 + Martinazzoli et al. prototypes), CMS HGCAL (TDR CERN-LHCC-2017-023, ~20–30 ps showers, self-branded '5D imaging'), ATLAS HGTD (CERN-LHCC-2020-007), SCEPCAL (Lucchini et al., JINST 15 (2020) P11005 — LYSO timing layers for FCC-ee). A NIM A reader cannot tell whether RADiCAL leads or trails the field.

[-] Prior-art vulnerability: D. Anderson et al., NIM A 794 (2015) 7, 'On timing properties of LYSO-based calorimeters' (Caltech: ~30 ps with LYSO+W sampling structures at FNAL, with R.-Y. Zhu in that author network — and Zhu/Zhang are co-authors of THIS paper) is the direct predecessor measurement and is uncited. Likewise Benaglia/Gundacker et al., NIM A 830 (2016) (sub-20 ps L(Y)SO+SiPM). A referee could reasonably allege unacknowledged priority.

[-] Methods canon absent: CFD/LED time-walk literature (Gedcke & McDonald, NIM 55 (1967)), digital waveform-timing taxonomy (Genat, Varner, Tang, Frisch, NIM A 607 (2009) 387), DRS4 timebase/calibration (Ritt NIM A 518 (2004); Stricker-Shaver, Ritt, Pichler IEEE TNS 61 (2014)) and MCP-PMT timing references for the R3809U-50 (Inami et al., NIM A 560 (2006); Ronzhin et al., NIM A 623 (2010)) are all missing — the '10 < sigma_t < 20 ps' MCP claim (Sec. 4) is uncited, and ref 23 is just the CAEN product URL. Refs 12–13 are non-archival Box links that will rot and cannot be verified by a referee.

[-] 'Shashlik' technique is used without its canon: no Atoian et al. (KOPIO shashlyk, NIM A 531 (2004) 467), no PHENIX EMCal (NIM A 499 (2003)), no LHCb/HERA-B shashlik ECALs — particularly damaging given Sec. 7.1 explicitly promises a position-localization paper that lives inside that literature, and the wire chamber used as truth is uncited (no Spanggaard, CERN SL-Note-98-023 DWC guide).
paper2_timing: BENCHMARK TABLE (the single highest-value addition): one table, technology vs sensor vs measured sigma_t vs conditions (MIP or EM shower, energy, reference, rad environment, channels per area). Rows: CMS BTL (LYSO+SiPM, 30->60 ps/MIP, MTD TDR), CMS ETL + ATLAS HGTD (LGAD, ~35–50 ps), CMS HGCAL Si (~20 ps high-E showers, beam-test papers e.g. Acar et al. JINST), CMS PbWO4 ECAL (~30 ps high-amplitude, JINST 5 (2010) T03011 + Phase-2 TDR), LHCb PicoCal SPACAL W-GAGG (~15–20 ps at >=80 GeV beam tests), MCP shower-max devices (Ronzhin/Apresyan NIM A 795 (2015)), RADiCAL published (27 ps cfd05) and THIS WORK (25.3 ps lgcfd brightest-1000, floor ~19–20 ps). Positioning sentence the table supports: 'sub-30 ps EM-shower timing from a 14x14 mm2, 16-SiPM-channel module' — the differentiator is channel economy + compactness + integrated E/t, NOT raw ps (SPACAL W-GAGG is faster; never claim outright best).

Engage LHCb SPACAL/PicoCal head-on in the text (not just the table): same W-absorber fast-timing EM goal, ~15–20 ps demonstrated; state explicitly what RADiCAL adds (shower-max-localized sampling, dual-end depth observable, LYSO radiation tolerance at FCC-hh doses citing Zhu-group LYSO rad-hardness IEEE TNS papers + Hu et al. ref 24, ultra-compact RM=13.7 mm). Silence on SPACAL is the #1 thing a knowledgeable NIM A referee will attack.

Frame the 8-source survey (cfd03..cfd50, led, lgcfd — the new timing_allmethods figures) inside the timing-methods canon: cite Gedcke & McDonald (CFD origin), Genat et al. NIM A 607 (2009) (digital LED/CFD/template taxonomy and why waveform sampling enables all of them offline), and the LGAD community's amplitude-walk corrections (Cartiglia et al., NIM A 850 (2017)). This converts the per-build method choice (lgcfd for bright/clipped, led for dim) from 'method shopping' into a literature-grounded regime statement, backed by the optimization appendix.

Position lgcfd's novelty honestly against related art: dual-gain/ToT saturation recovery is established in front-end design (HGCAL HGCROC ToT, ATLAS LAr multi-gain — cite TDRs) and time-walk correction is standard in TOF-PET (Cates ref 22 already in hand; add Gundacker et al. PMB CTR reviews). Claim: first application of LG-predicted-peak CFD de-saturation to beam-test shower timing, demonstrated to remove the published 0.2 ns satellite (their Figs. 18–19) and lower the floor 22->19.5 ps on identical events (method_compare figure). Related-art acknowledgment is what makes the novelty claim survive review.

Anchor the light-yield thesis (money plot, light_yield_thesis.png) to the established CTR scaling sigma_t ~ sqrt(tau_r*tau_d/N_pe) (Gundacker et al., Phys. Med. Biol. reviews; Cates 2018): overlay the predicted a ~ 1/sqrt(LY) on the four-build stochastic terms. Cite LuAG:Ce kinetics (Hu et al. ref 24 + Crystal Clear LuAG-fiber beam tests, Lucchini et al. JINST 8 (2013) P10017) to QUANTIFY the kinetics confound, then present the MIXED in-event kill-shot (ratio 0.99) as the in-situ resolution of exactly that confound. This turns 'we measured 0.99' into 'photostatistics theory confirmed in a shower-max calorimeter'.

DRS4/reference credibility block: cite Ritt (DRS4) and Stricker-Shaver/Ritt/Pichler (sub-ps SCA calibration) when explaining the 71–74 ps inter-group domino jitter, the stop-cell recovery, and why same-group (DW-UP)/2 cancels it exactly; cite Inami et al. NIM A 560 (2006) / Va'vra MCP timing for the R3809U-50 reference budget. Replace Box-link refs 12–13 with archival equivalents (arXiv/Instruments versions, refs 14–16 family) — non-archival links in a methods-heavy paper invite distrust.

WLS/capillary heritage paragraph: Ruchti, Annu. Rev. Nucl. Part. Sci. 46 (1996) 281 (scintillating-fiber/capillary tracking lineage), the DSB1 development record in citable form (upgrade OSTI ref 21 to the actual report number; Eljen as supplier note, not a reference), and the published paper's own Figs. 3–4 as the bench baseline the in-beam comparison extends (explicitly fulfilling its Sec. 7.2 promise).

Floor physics with external anchors: present b ~ 19–20 ps as shower-max depth fluctuation (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 1 X0 = 5.4 mm), citing the PDG EM-shower longitudinal profile (t_max ~ ln(E/Ec)) and a GEANT4 cross-check, and note CONFIRMATION of the published 17.5 ps (Eq. 2) — never a revision. Cite dual-readout timing-structure work (RD52/Akchurin) as precedent for extracting shower-development information from calorimeter time.
paper3_energy_position: Position-sensitive shashlik benchmark table: Atoian et al. (KOPIO shashlyk, NIM A 531 (2004) 467: ~3%/sqrtE, few-mm position), PHENIX EMCal (NIM A 499 (2003)), LHCb/HERA-B shashlik ECALs, COMPASS — columns: cell size, position method (inter-cell cluster CoG vs intra-cell light division), sigma_x at high E. RADiCAL's claim is the regime nobody else occupies: INTRA-module 4-capillary light-division at shower max inside a single 14 mm cell, ~1.5 mm residual at 150 GeV. Without the table the 1.5 mm number has no context; with it, the novelty is self-evident.

Wire-chamber comparator honesty WITH the citation referees expect: CERN delay-wire chambers are canonically ~200 um devices (Spanggaard, SL-Note-98-023) — the measured 3.6 mm here is digitizer-limited (1 GS/s DRS1 readout of the delay lines, wireChamberResolution.C). State this explicitly and cite Spanggaard, or a DWC-savvy referee will conclude the analysis is wrong. Then present 1.5 mm (x: 1.52, y: 1.44) and the 4D demo's 0.9 mm as COMPARATOR-LIMITED UPPER LIMITS (also below the 2.9 mm beam spot), with the >=3.5–4 mm binning guardrail applied in every position-dependent figure.

Energy-resolution framing that survives juxtaposition: the 52%/sqrtE + 31.6%/E + 9.3% (published Eq. 1) and the per-build ~14% @150 GeV are SHOWER-MAX SAMPLING numbers from ~3 LYSO tiles, not calorimeter resolution — repeat the published paper's own disclaimer loudly, cite Wigmans (Calorimetry, OUP) for sampling-fluctuation theory and the shashlik canon (KOPIO 3%/sqrtE, LHCb ~10%/sqrtE) as the full-module comparators, and show the GEANT4 full-module/3x3-array expectation against the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% program goal. The WLS-species-independence of sigma_E/E (13.5–15% across DSB1/LuAG/MIXED) is a publishable result only when framed as sampling-and-leakage-dominated.

E-type characterization positioned as the in-beam analog of published Fig. 4 (bench LED uniformity), with the T/E division of labour related to shashlik WLS-fiber readout uniformity literature (Atoian) and LuAG-fiber calorimetry (Lucchini et al. JINST 8 (2013) P10017). Quote the slopes (1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV), linearity, and the constant-term-dominated per-cap resolution; note explicitly that no 4xE-type configuration exists in this dataset (only the one-off run 2247), so E-type conclusions are single-capillary.

Granularity contrast for the localization claim: cite ALICE FoCal TDR (CERN-LHCC-2024-004) and CMS HGCAL TDR as the high-granularity route to mm-scale EM localization, then position RADiCAL as the channel-economical compact alternative (16 SiPMs/module) achieving comparable transverse localization via light division — plus the module-ID/overlapping-shower pattern-recognition argument the published paper already sketches (its Fig. 9 and Sec. 3).

Depth observable cross-disciplinary anchor: (DW-UP)/2 is dual-ended depth-of-interaction readout transplanted from PET (cite dual-ended-readout DOI literature, e.g. Y. Yang et al., Phys. Med. Biol.) to EM shower max; cite ATLAS LAr strip pointing / CMS photon pointing for why per-shower z matters at colliders. This is the bridge figure (depth_corr/absShowerTime) literature home.

4D framing citations: SCEPCAL (Lucchini et al., JINST 15 (2020) P11005) for the crystal-timing-layer 4D vision at future colliders, HGCAL TDR for the existing '5D imaging' branding, CMS MTD TDR for the 4D-vertexing physics case (pileup ~140–200, PU~1000 at FCC-hh from refs 1–5). The TENERGY capstone figure then claims '4D in ONE ultra-compact module' — a claim no cited competitor makes at this form factor.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: An honest, demonstrated 4D claim: simultaneous per-event measurement of (x, y, E, t) at EM shower max in a SINGLE 14x14x135 mm3 TENERGY module from one 150 GeV event sample — sigma_t ~ 57 ps all-fiducial (~35 ps best-bin) via MCP-free (DW-UP)/2 on 3 T-type capillaries, sigma_E/E ~ 11.9% (shower-max sampling, scoped as such), position residual ~0.9–1.5 mm (explicitly tracker-comparator-limited, WC ~3.6 mm, beam spot 2.9 mm), all on a co-registered frame (module center, 0.79 mm MCP alignment). Extendable to a multi-energy 4D demonstration (50–150 GeV, 5 points) with existing data. Additionally claimable: the module carries a depth-SENSITIVE observable ((DW-UP)/2 proportional to longitudinal light origin; sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4–6 mm ~ 1 X0) — i.e., '4D demonstrated, with a path to a fifth coordinate', positioned against SCEPCAL (proposed, not beam-demonstrated) and HGCAL (5D via ~6M channels vs 16 SiPMs here).
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Cannot claim '5D calorimeter' today: there is no calibrated per-event z measurement — (DW-UP)/2 is currently used as the timing estimator whose SPREAD is the floor, with no z calibration constant derived from data, no resolution quoted in mm against an independent depth truth, and no demonstration that the depth estimate is unbiased vs energy. Cannot claim competitive standalone energy resolution (52%/sqrtE + 9.3% is shower-max sampling only; the 10%/sqrtE goal is unmet by design in this configuration). Cannot claim 1.5/0.9 mm as intrinsic position resolution (comparator- and beam-spot-limited). Cannot claim array-level 4D reconstruction or hadron/pileup performance — single module, electrons only, 25–150 GeV. Cannot use HGCAL-style 'imaging' language: one longitudinal sample (shower max) is not imaging.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Data-only, no new hardware: calibrate (DW-UP)/2 into millimeters and validate it against the KNOWN physics of shower-max depth. The mean shower-max depth grows as X0*ln(E) (PDG profile): across 25->150 GeV the mean of (DW-UP)/2 should shift by ~X0*ln(6)/(2*v_fiber) ~ 24 ps — directly measurable in the existing DSB1 six-energy reduced data. If the per-energy MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 tracks ln(E) with slope consistent with v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns (GEANT4 cross-check, building on the published Fig. 7 simulation), the depth observable is CALIBRATED in mm and an honest claim becomes available: 'single-module 5D estimator (x, y, z, E, t) with sigma_z ~ 1 X0 per event'. Quote it as 'toward 5D' / a measured fifth coordinate with ~5 mm resolution — never as a 5D imaging calorimeter.

killer_additions: The two benchmark tables (fast-timing technologies for Paper 2; position-sensitive shashlik heritage for Paper 3), each with a conditions column (MIP vs shower, energy, reference subtraction, channel count, rad environment). No published compact-calorimeter paper currently puts BTL, HGCAL, SPACAL W-GAGG, LGADs, MCP shower-max and a shashlik-timing module in one honest table — these tables would make the RADiCAL papers the citable reference point for the whole sub-30 ps EM-timing niche, and they cost only literature work, not new analysis.

The ln(E) depth-calibration measurement: show the per-energy MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 across 25–150 GeV tracking X0*ln(E)/(2*v_fiber) (~24 ps predicted shift), overlaid with GEANT4 shower-max depth — one figure that simultaneously (a) proves the floor IS shower-depth physics (closing Paper 2's floor argument with data instead of consistency arithmetic), (b) calibrates the fifth coordinate in mm for Paper 3's 'toward 5D' claim, and (c) imports the PET dual-ended DOI literature as a memorable cross-disciplinary hook ('depth-of-interaction readout for EM showers').

The theory-anchored money plot: overlay the Gundacker/Cates CTR photostatistics scaling (a proportional to 1/sqrt(LY)) on the four-build stochastic terms, with the MIXED in-event kill-shot (DSB1/LuAG ratio 0.99, chi2/ndf 0.4/5) presented as the confound-free in-situ test of that scaling law — elevating Paper 2 from 'a materials comparison' to 'the controlled experiment that confirms light-transport-limited timing in shower-max calorimetry', which is the sentence reviewers and citers will remember.
referee_risks: 'Why no comparison to LHCb SPACAL W-GAGG (~15–20 ps) or CMS BTL (same LYSO+SiPM)?' — the single most likely major-revision demand for Paper 2; any 'best' or 'unprecedented' timing wording without the benchmark table will be struck. Pre-empt by claiming the compactness/channel-economy/integrated-E+t niche, not raw picoseconds.

Prior-art objection: Anderson et al., NIM A 794 (2015) (LYSO-based calorimeter timing, ~30 ps, Zhu author network) uncited while Zhu/Zhang are on the author roster — a referee noticing this will question the literature review wholesale. Also: non-archival Box-link references (parent refs 12–13) must not reappear in Papers 2–3.

Position-resolution kill-shot: quoting 1.5 mm (or the 4D demo's 0.9 mm) below both the 3.6 mm wire-chamber resolution and the 2.9 mm beam spot will be rejected as impossible unless framed as comparator-limited; and DWC-literate referees expect ~200 um trackers (Spanggaard), so the digitizer-limited WC readout needs explicit explanation and citation, plus the >=3.5–4 mm binning guardrail in every position figure.

Energy-resolution juxtaposition trap: 52%/sqrtE + 9.3% printed near the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% program goal reads as 'misses own target by 5x' unless every instance carries the shower-max-sampling scope and a GEANT4 full-module/3x3 projection; reviewers of Paper 3 will test this boundary repeatedly.

'Method shopping' accusation: per-build timing-source choice (lgcfd for DSB1/MIXED, led for LuAG/TENERGY) plus the deliberately-saturated HG strategy will be challenged as tuned-per-result; defenses must ship in-paper — the optimization appendix (adopted source below the entire CFD family per build), the locked brightest-1000 selection with OOS folding, the <=1.5 ps selection-systematics table, and literature framing (Genat et al. taxonomy; dual-gain/ToT saturation-recovery precedents) — otherwise the +3.1 ps method gain looks like cherry-picking.

MIXED kill-shot provenance: the corner material map (NE+SW DSB1, NW+SE LuAG) is data-inferred and flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook in dataset_2023.md; the in-event 0.99 ratio — Paper 2's central evidentiary claim — rests on it. Confirm from the logbook before submission, or a referee question ('how do you know which corner is which?') has no documented answer. Same for the LuAG-build NW capillary type ambiguity.

4D/5D terminology policing: HGCAL already self-describes as 5D imaging and SCEPCAL owns the crystal-4D vision; an unqualified '5D calorimeter' claim from one module with an uncalibrated depth observable will be attacked as overreach. Use '4D demonstration in a single module' + 'depth-sensitive observable, sigma_z ~ 1 X0' until the ln(E) calibration figure exists; cite both competitors when defining terms.

Floor wording: any sentence reading as a revision of the published 17.5 ps (Eq. 2) invites self-contradiction attacks across the paper series; all floor numbers must be presented as per-technique, form-dependent extrapolations that CONFIRM the published value (photostat 1/sqrtE fit, b = 19.5 +/- 1.1 stat +/- 1.2 syst), with the measured 25.3 ps @150 GeV as the headline and the caveat that LuAG never reaches the extrapolated floor in data.

experts.lens: Coordinating multi-channel waveform analysis for fast timing: per-channel crossing extraction, per-event channel combination, inter-calibration, vetoes, reference subtraction, clipped-vs-unclipped regime handling, time-walk, and optimality bounds (template/ML baselines).
paper1_assessment: [+] The six-method ladder (Fig 27 Left: Downstream/Upstream/BestPlus/BestPlus-MCP/(DW-UP)/2/BestMinus) is a genuine combination taxonomy, and the (DW-UP)/2 self-referencing differential is the right multi-channel design: both ends carry the same per-group MCP copy and DRS4 domino phase, so the differential cancels reference jitter, beam-arrival jitter, and common-mode edge shape by construction. The channel map (NW/NE/SE corners wholly in DRS0 G0 with MCP1) makes this cancellation exact - a deliberate wiring choice, though the paper never explains WHY the differential beats the absolute methods (it shows it empirically in Fig 27 but attributes the gap only to 'MCP resolution').

[+] The amplitude-binned analysis (Figs 21-26, nine equal bins per energy) honestly exposes sigma_t(amplitude), and the deliberate-saturation strategy (drive HG into clipping to steepen the recorded foot, Sec 4) paired with a fixed-voltage LED threshold is internally consistent: a fixed-mV crossing is immune to the unknown clipped peak, where any fraction-of-peak CFD would not be. The ~0.2 ns satellite (Fig 19, ~15% of signals, one-sample digitization shift) is documented rather than hidden.

[-] The per-event combination is a plain unweighted mean of 4+4 ends (Delta_t_DW = Sigma Delta_t(i)/4) with no per-channel inter-calibration of time offsets, no resolution weighting (1/sigma_i^2), no in-event channel-consistency veto, and no handling of the known weak channel (NW-Up; dropping it improves the combination by ~10 ps per the 2023 reanalysis). The 15% one-sample satellite population is exactly what a channel-consistency veto removes, yet it is waved off as 'little effect' with no quantified bound. No time-walk correction is applied or even discussed for the fixed-threshold LED on pulses spanning 15% amplitude spreads.

[-] The headline fit sigma_t = 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps (Eq 2, Fig 27 Right) carries no uncertainties on a or b, no chi2, no systematics, and b=17.5 ps is an extrapolation beyond the 150 GeV data presented as a measured limit ('in the limit of high energy'). The BestMinus selection is an in-sample argmin over equal-width amplitude bins 6-8 - the one selection the 2023 OOS audit shows genuinely overfits (in-sample 25.6 -> OOS 27.5 ps); the published 27 ps survives only because the equal-width argmin happens to land near the honest OOS value. No out-of-sample validation, no floor decomposition (slew vs digitizer inter-group jitter ~71-74 ps vs shower-depth fluctuation ~1 X0), and BestPlus-MCP subtracts 'sigma_t,MCP ~ 10 ps' when the dominant reference contribution in the absolute methods is actually the inter-group DRS4 domino jitter, not the MCP intrinsic.
paper2_timing: A formal method-taxonomy table and survey figure: per-channel crossing source (cfd03/05/10/20/30/50, led, lgcfd) x per-event combination (plain 4+4 mean, 1/sigma^2-weighted mean, drop-NW-Up, per-material diagonals for MIXED) x event selection (brightest-K, quantile best-bin, all-fiducial) x sigma extraction (Gaussian core / tebSigma robust fallback). Present the published method explicitly as ONE CELL of this grid and the adopted per-build cells highlighted by light regime. The timing_allmethods_{DSB1,LUAG,MIXED,TENERGY}.png survey already exists - elevate it to a main figure with a/sqrtE+b fits per cell.

An ALGORITHMIC regime rule, not per-build hand-picking: plot HG clipped fraction vs E per build (DSB1 4%@25 -> 93-94%@100-150; LuAG never clips) and state the decision rule 'clip fraction above X (or light level above Y) -> lgcfd; else led'. This is the only defensible answer to 'why does each build get a different method?' and it is exactly WHY a single module-wide method fails on MIXED (two corners clip, two do not).

The lgcfd edge-recovery method section with its full calibration discipline: per-channel HG=a+b*LG fit on UNCLIPPED low-E events, median-of-sane-slopes sidecar, slope energy-independence <=2%, frac=0.15 sweet spot (higher fracs re-hit the clip), recovered edge 3.7x steeper (381 vs 102 mV/ns), and the demonstrated-in-data satellite removal (cfd05-vs-lgcfd distribution overlay - a checkable claim, currently only asserted).

Per-channel inter-calibration and weighted combination as an explicit subsection: measured per-end constant offsets applied; per-channel sigma_i table per build; 1/sigma_i^2-weighted (DW-UP)/2 vs plain mean quantified (expected gain small for matched channels, ~10 ps when NW-Up is weak - show it). Then the in-event channel-consistency veto: characterize the ~30-sigma broken-timing outlier rate vs E/build, show it caused the fit-window inflation bug (the monotonicity fix), and prove the veto does not sculpt the core (sigma before/after with efficiency quoted).

Time-walk treatment: t-vs-amplitude walk curves per source; show cfd05-on-brightest walk (the documented clipping limitation motivating lgcfd) and bound the residual walk of led on dim builds (<1-2 ps or apply a correction). The HG-quality ranking trap (brightest HG = most clipped = worst timing; LG-sum ranking correct) deserves one explicit paragraph - referees from MIP-timing communities will assume amplitude ranking.

The reference-budget section: (DW-UP)/2 self-referencing vs MCP-referenced absolute time, with the measured sigma(MCP1-MCP2)/sqrt2 = 71-74 ps flat-vs-E figure proving it is inter-group domino jitter (amplitude-independent, MCP intrinsic ~10 ps common-mode and cancelled), and the channel-map design that keeps the headline below it. This corrects the parent paper's muddled '~10 ps MCP subtraction' narrative without attacking it.

MIXED per-material diagonals as the mandatory analysis (DSB1 corners lgcfd vs LuAG corners led within the SAME showers) plus the in-event pairwise-solve kill-shot (ratio 0.99, chi2/ndf 0.4/5) with the cancellation algebra spelled out. Prerequisite: confirm the data-inferred corner-material map against the logbook (currently flagged CONFIRM in dataset_2023.md) - the kill-shot stands on it.

An optimality bound to pre-empt 'why no ML?': (a) the analytic Cramer-Rao/slew bound per build from measured edge slope and noise (foot 22->12 ps, steep 7.5->2.1 ps single-channel; ~2 ps for (DW-UP)/2) vs achieved sigma - showing the floor is shower-depth physics, not extraction inefficiency; (b) at least one template-fit or small-NN/GBDT spot-check on rising-edge samples at one energy showing <=1-2 ps gain over lgcfd. Cite optimal filtering (Cleland-Stern), CMS MTD/HGCAL timing, and waveform-NN timing literature. Without this, an instrumentation referee will ask whether the waveform information was exhausted.

OOS honesty published as a method contribution: matched-fraction fold-by-run validation, the equal-width-argmin-overfits demonstration, and the framing that the published 27.4 ps best-bin IS the honest OOS value (confirm, never revise 17.5 ps); floors quoted per-technique, free-fit with PDG scaling, both 1/sqrtE and 1/E forms, headline = MEASURED 25.3 ps @150 GeV.

TENERGY in the survey with the 3-capillary (DW-UP)/2 documented: E-type exclusion rule stated, the sqrt(4/3) statistical penalty expectation vs measured, and a check that the asymmetric 3-corner geometry does not bias the differential.
paper3_energy_position: RECONCILE THE POSITION COMPARATOR FIRST: the quoted residuals (1.5 mm at 150 GeV; 0.9 mm in the 4D demo) sit BELOW the wire chamber's own measured ~3.6 mm resolution. A residual cannot be smaller than the comparator unless the TLinearFitter trained on WC truth absorbed correlated smearing or the WC sigma is overestimated. Either re-derive the WC resolution (the 3.3 mm sub-sample CFD-50% number vs the self-cal 3.6 mm), present sigma_residual^2 = sigma_cap^2 + sigma_WC^2 with the implied capillary bound, or train/evaluate on disjoint event sets. As written this is the single most attackable number in the paper.

Position estimator systematics from the waveform side: the center-of-gravity/linear-fraction estimator has the standard S-curve/pincushion nonlinearity near edges - show the bias map vs (x,y), the energy dependence of the residual, and obey the >=3.5-4 mm binning guardrail (WC quantizes at ~0.25 mm/sample) so position-dependent plots are not smearing-correlated. Document the WC delay-line timing chain itself (50% CFD at 1 GS/s, sub-sample interpolation) as a timing measurement with its own budget.

Energy chain inter-calibration documented as in the parent paper but per build: per-channel LG gain equalization (parent Fig 14; UPSE low gain), linearity (29.4 mV/GeV parent normalization vs the ~55 mV/GeV LG-sum number - reconcile the two conventions explicitly), peak-vs-charge choice given the AC-coupled ~35% undershoot, Pb-glass leakage linearization (9.2% -> 2.2%), and the 4-population containment classification with the SumPb < 0.30 SumLG cut efficiency (95.3% @150).

Keep the parent paper's shower-max disclaimer load-bearing: sigma_E/E ~ 14% @150 is restricted-transverse-sampling (~3 tiles ~ 1 R_M), NOT full-module resolution and NOT comparable to the 10%/sqrtE program goal. The new result is the multi-build comparison (13.5-15% WLS-species-independent) - energy at shower max is sampling/leakage-limited, insensitive to capillary fill.

E-type characterization as the in-beam analog of parent Fig 4: linearity 1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV, ~4-5x dimmer, single-channel timing comparable to T-type, energy resolution constant-term dominated and near-flat 50-150 GeV; the T/E division of labour stated as geometry+role, not WLS species. Note no four-E-type configuration exists (one-off LuAG run 2247 aside) - scope claims accordingly.

The depth observable promoted properly: (DW-UP)/2 as longitudinal depth with the in-data scale validation (see killer additions) and v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns; the depth-corrected absolute-time estimator (T_abs = mean(hg_led), MCP-referenced, ~50 ps) as the route below the ~20 ps floor - this requires the reducer-level absolute-timebase fix + re-reduction before it is a publishable figure, so either finish that or scope it as outlook.

The 4D capstone (TENERGY, 150 GeV) with co-registration honesty: shared frame (module center 6.58/4.66 mm, MCP-RADiCAL alignment 0.79 mm), one event sample, one module; quote sigma_t = 57 ps all-fiducial with the ~35 ps best-bin as the selection-dependent best case, and use DISTINCT observables for time and depth (see verdict) so the dimensions are not double-counted.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: A demonstrated simultaneous 4D (x, y, E, t) measurement at EM shower max in ONE module from ONE event sample: TENERGY at 150 GeV gives sigma_t ~ 57 ps all-fiducial (~35 ps best-bin) via the MCP-free (DW-UP)/2 on 3 T-type capillaries, sigma_E/E ~ 11.9% (shower-max sampling), and a transverse residual ~0.9-1.5 mm vs the wire chamber (tracker-comparator-limited). Plus the supporting claim that the same four corner amplitudes deliver energy AND position, and the timing floor is itself a depth observable - the 5th dimension is PRESENT in the data.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Not '5D demonstrated' as an event-level depth MEASUREMENT: (DW-UP)/2 has no independent per-event depth truth in this dataset (its spread matching ~1 X0 is a consistency check, not a validation), and the current 4D demo uses (DW-UP)/2 as 'the time' - using the same linear combination as both t and depth double-counts one observable as two dimensions. Also cannot claim program goals met (10 ps, 10%/sqrtE - the energy number is shower-max sampling only), cannot claim a calibrated 1.5 mm position resolution (comparator-limited, and currently inconsistent with the WC sigma), and the 4D demo is one energy, one module.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Three cheap analyses unlock an honest 5D-at-shower-max claim: (1) the in-data depth-scale calibration - the MEAN of (DW-UP)/2 must shift with ln(E) as X_max walks deeper (~1.8 X0 ~ 9.7 mm ~ 48 ps from 25 to 150 GeV on DSB1; parent Fig 7 already shows the GEANT4 walk from layers 8-10 to 11-13) - a measured mean-vs-lnE slope consistent with v_fiber ~ 200 mm/ns validates the depth scale with no simulation truth; (2) report t and depth as INDEPENDENT combinations: t = MCP-referenced mean(hg_led) (~50 ps absolute), depth z = v_fiber*(DW-UP)/2 with sigma_z ~ 4-5.6 mm ~ 1 X0 after subtracting the instrumental jitter in quadrature; (3) a GEANT4 per-event depth-fluctuation width vs the measured (DW-UP)/2 width per energy. With those, '5D information (x, y, z_shower, E, t) from one shower-max module' is defensible; without them, claim 4D + 'depth sensitivity demonstrated'.

killer_additions: The depth-scale money plot: mean (DW-UP)/2 vs ln(E) across 25-150 GeV on all builds, overlaid with the X_max ~ X0*ln(E) prediction (slope ~ X0/v_fiber ~ 27 ps per e-fold, ~48 ps total shift). One figure simultaneously validates v_fiber in-data, proves the floor IS shower-depth physics (not an excuse), turns the timing floor into a measured 5th dimension, and connects parent Fig 7 (GEANT4 layer walk) to beam data. No other group with a single-ended shashlik can make this plot - dual-ended readout is the differentiator.

A near-optimality bound figure: per build, the analytic Cramer-Rao/slew limit from measured edge slope and pedestal noise vs the achieved sigma_t for every method cell, plus one template-fit/NN spot-check at one energy. It converts 'we used simple thresholds' from a weakness into a theorem ('the simple recovered-edge methods sit within ~2 ps of the waveform information limit; the residual is shower physics') and pre-empts the inevitable ML referee.

The method 'periodic table': a single grid figure - crossing source x combination x selection - colored by sigma_t(150), with the published LED+plain-mean cell, the adopted lgcfd/led cells, and the MIXED per-material diagonal cells highlighted. It makes the whole multi-build, multi-regime story legible in one glance and gives other groups (CMS MTD, HGCAL, future FCC R&D) a reusable decision framework for clipped-vs-unclipped multi-channel timing.
referee_risks: Position residual (0.9-1.5 mm) quoted below the wire chamber's own ~3.6 mm resolution - internally inconsistent as stated; will be caught immediately. Reconcile (correlation via fit training, WC sigma re-derivation, or quadrature decomposition) before submission.

Per-build method switching (lgcfd for DSB1/MIXED, led for LuAG/TENERGY) reads as cherry-picking unless the algorithmic clip-fraction/light-level regime rule is stated and the full survey shows every method on every build with the adopted one winning for a stated physical reason.

The MIXED kill-shot (ratio 0.99) rests on a DATA-INFERRED corner material map flagged 'CONFIRM against logbook' - and the LuAG-build NW capillary type is also unconfirmed. Both must be logbook-verified; a misassigned corner kills the paper's central proof.

Floor claims: b is an extrapolation beyond 150 GeV; LuAG's brightest measured point is still ~42 ps, so 'material-independent ~20 ps floor' must be presented as a form-dependent extrapolation (1/sqrtE vs 1/E both shown) with free-fit errors, never as a measurement - and never as a revision of the published 17.5 ps.

The OOS audit shows the parent paper's equal-width best-bin argmin overfits in-sample - frame as 'the published number is the honest OOS value and stands' (confirmation), not as an indictment of a paper sharing 39 authors with this one.

The in-event channel-consistency veto and the satellite removal must come with quoted efficiencies and a no-sculpting demonstration; 'we vetoed outliers' without rates vs E/build invites a bias accusation.
Absence of any template-fit/ML baseline or analytic bound invites 'you used a threshold from the 1980s on 5 GS/s waveforms' - the CRLB figure is the cheap insurance.

The 4D demo is one energy (150 GeV), one module, with t taken from the same combination claimed as depth - scope the claim and split the observables, or a careful referee will note the dimensions are not independent.

experts.lens: HEP phenomenology — physics cases (pi0/gamma separation at shower max, H->gamma gamma timing-vertexing, LLP/delayed-photon searches, forward physics, FCC-hh pile-up and muon-collider BIB rejection) and the quantitative bridges from this dataset's measured numbers to citable physics reach.
paper1_assessment: [+] Eq. 2 / Fig. 27 give the one thing phenomenologists can actually use: a closed-form sigma_t(E) = 256/sqrtE + 17.5 ps over 25-150 GeV, the exact parametric format fast-sim and Delphes-style studies plug in; the energy range maps onto H->gamma gamma photon energies at FCC-hh (boosted/forward) and fixed-target spectra.

[+] The shower-max localization concept is correctly seeded for phenomenology: GEANT4 Fig. 9 shows the timing/energy signal comes from r <= 5 mm at shower max, and Sec. 3 explicitly names the high-eta/forward overlapping-shower pattern-recognition use case — the germ of a pi0/gamma and module-ID story, with Fig. 28 the upgrade path (5 T-type + 4 E-type, 18x18 mm).

[-] No physics-reach translation anywhere: 27 ps is never converted to a vertex-z (c*27 ps ~ 8 mm vs the ~45-50 mm HL-LHC/FCC luminous region), a pile-up time-slicing factor (vs the ~160-180 ps FCC-hh collision-time spread), or a BIB timing window. Sec. 8's claim 'potential to meet and exceed the timing requirements for FCC-ee and FCC-hh' is asserted with no quantitative chain — so the paper is citable only for the raw sigma_t, not for any physics case.

[-] The headline BestMinus (DW-UP)/2 (Fig. 27) is a self-referenced DIFFERENTIAL whose spread is shower-depth fluctuation; the collider-relevant absolute time-of-arrival vs an external clock (BestPlus-MCP, Fig. 27 Left) is plotted but the conceptual distinction (depth observable vs ToF observable) is never explained. A phenomenologist reading '27 ps' as photon ToF for H->gamma gamma vertexing or BIB windows would over-claim — and a careful referee of the follow-ups will notice.

[-] Eq. 1 (52.04%/sqrtE + 31.62%/E + 9.31%) is honestly caveated as shower-max-only sampling, but no projection connects it to the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% program goal (Sec. 1); the 9.31% constant term invites mis-citation as 'the RADiCAL energy resolution'. Position localization — the third leg of the 10 ps / 1 mm / 10%/sqrtE pitch — is entirely deferred to Sec. 7.1 with no measured number.

[-] Radiation hardness motivates the whole forward/FCC-hh pitch (Sec. 1, Sec. 7.3 'LuAG:Ce favored for ... excellent radiation hardness [14,24]'), but the paper carries no dose/fluence quantification and the rad-hard-WLS timing comparison is deferred — the physics case most cited for compact W/LYSO technology is left as a promise.
paper2_timing: NECESSARY: an explicit differential-vs-absolute timing taxonomy with BOTH measured numbers per build. (DW-UP)/2 = clock-free, cancels reference/timebase — its 25.3 ps (DSB1, lgcfd, brightest-1000 @150 GeV) is depth-fluctuation-limited and is what a self-timed calorimeter cell delivers; the absolute shower time vs MCP (mean hg_led, ~50 ps in this data) is the ToF-relevant quantity for H->gamma gamma vertexing, delayed photons, and BIB windows. State which physics case uses which observable. Omitting this is the single biggest mis-citation risk.

NECESSARY: a short 'physics context' section translating the per-build fits into reach: c*sigma_t = 0.3 mm/ps -> 25 ps = 7.6 mm path-length discrimination; two-photon H->gamma gamma vertex-z ~ c*sigma_t/sqrt(2) ~ 5-8 mm against a ~45-50 mm luminous region (cite CMS MTD TDR benchmark of 30-40 ps); FCC-hh PU~1000 with ~170 ps collision-time spread -> a 25 ps detector slices effective vertex density by ~x7 (label as extrapolation); muon-collider BIB: a +/-2sigma = +/-50 ps window vs the ns-scale BIB arrival spread (cite IMCC/Crilin 20-40 ps targets). All numbers flow from the measured a/sqrtE + b fits — no new data needed.

NECESSARY: frame the MIXED kill-shot (in-event DSB1 vs LuAG:Ce per-capillary ratio 0.99, chi2/ndf 0.4/5) as THE rad-hardness result phenomenologists will cite: the radiation-hard ceramic WLS forfeits NO intrinsic timing — only light yield (stochastic a: 201 -> 455 ps*GeV^1/2; floor b ~ 20 ps shared). Conclusion for FCC-hh forward / GRad environments: timing recovery is a light-budget engineering problem (2x LYSO thickness at shower max per parent Sec. 7.3), not a materials physics penalty. Cite C. Hu et al. NIM A 954 (2020) 161723 plus LuAG/LYSO fluence-tolerance literature and the HGCAL/BTL dose specs for context.

NECESSARY: present the ~19.5 ps floor as shower-depth physics with the conversion shown (sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t, 200 mm/ns: 25-28 ps -> 5.0-5.6 mm ~ 1 X0 = 5.4 mm), then state the corollary both ways: (a) the floor is irreducible for this estimator and material-independent by geometry; (b) it is therefore itself a depth MEASUREMENT (the 5D hook, handed to the energy+position paper), and the depth-corrected absolute estimator is the route below 20 ps toward the 10 ps program target. Follow the floor-honesty rules: headline measured 25.3 ps @150, quote b as a form-dependent extrapolation (1/sqrtE 19.5 +/- 1.1 vs 1/E 26.1), and state LuAG's brightest measured point is still ~42 ps.

NECESSARY: a benchmark table against contemporaries so the paper becomes the comparison phenomenologists pull numbers from: CMS MTD BTL ~30-60 ps (BoL-EoL), CMS ECAL ~30 ps high-E, HGCAL few-10s ps for showers, LHCb ECAL upgrade ~15-20 ps target, Crilin/muon-collider 20-40 ps — vs RADiCAL 25-27 ps measured at 150 GeV in a 14x14x135 mm^3, ~1 R_M module with clock-free self-referencing. Compactness per unit timing is the differentiator; say it quantitatively.

NECESSARY: one master table of (a, b) with stat+syst per build x method (cfd05/led/lgcfd), tied to the now-monotonic adopted-method curves and the <=1.5 ps selection systematics — this is the table that gets transcribed into fast-sim cards. Include the timing-method survey honesty: lgcfd for bright/clipped, led for dim, and the cfd05 time-walk failure on brightest events as the documented motivation.

WORDING/VALIDATION: state explicitly that 25-150 GeV ELECTRONS are the testbeam proxy for photons (no conversion/shower-start systematic studied) and that all collider-reach numbers are extrapolations from a single module at normal incidence; this preempts the standard pheno-referee objection.
paper3_energy_position: NECESSARY (the citable position result): convert position into pi0/gamma separation reach. Give the formula E_max ~ 2*m_pi0*L/s_min and evaluate for representative lever arms (collider EM face L ~ 2 m: minimum gamma-gamma separation 3.6 mm at 150 GeV; forward L ~ 10 m: 18 mm > one module width). With 14 mm (~1 R_M) modules + mm-level intra-module centroids, state up to what photon energy two showers are resolvable at each L. This is the single number a phenomenologist would cite from the position paper.

NECESSARY (and the dataset supports it): a data-driven two-shower study by EVENT MIXING — overlay pairs of single-electron events at wire-chamber-known impact offsets, re-run the 4-corner centroid/TLinearFitter reconstruction, and map two-shower identification efficiency and centroid pull vs separation (few mm to ~1 module) and energy. Single-electron beams cannot measure pi0 rejection directly; event mixing is the honest, reviewable surrogate and would be the paper's most-reproduced figure.

NECESSARY (position honesty): present the 1.5 mm (1.52 x / 1.44 y @150 GeV) residual — and the 0.9 mm 4D-demo residual — as TRACKER-COMPARATOR-LIMITED: the delay-line wire chamber itself measures sigma ~ 3.6 mm (wireChamberResolution.C), so the capillary estimator is demonstrably <= 1.5 mm but not calibrated at 1.5 mm. Respect the >= 3.5-4 mm binning guardrail in any position-dependent figure. Claiming '0.9 mm position resolution' outright is the most attackable statement in the paper.

NECESSARY (energy honesty + projection): keep sigma_E/E ~ 14% @150 GeV (13.5-15% across DSB1/LuAG/MIXED — WLS-species-independent) strictly labeled as shower-max sampling (~3 LYSO tiles, ~1 R_M transverse), consistent with the published 52%/sqrtE + 9.31%; then add the bridge the parent paper omitted — a GEANT4-anchored (or at minimum Sec.-7.3-argument) projection of full-module + 3x3-array resolution toward the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% goal, so the 9-14% numbers cannot be mis-cited as the technology's energy resolution.

NECESSARY (E-type): the first in-beam E-type characterization is genuinely new — quantify it as the energy element of the Fig.-28 enhanced module: 4-5x dimmer (1.6 vs 6.7 mV/GeV) but linear 50-150 GeV, constant-term-dominated per-capillary resolution, single-channel timing comparable to T-types; conclude the T/E division of labor is geometry/role, not WLS species.

NECESSARY (4D capstone, framed for physics): the TENERGY 150 GeV same-event demonstration — sigma_t ~ 57 ps all-fiducial (~35 ps best-bin, 3 T-type caps), sigma_E/E ~ 11.9% (shower-max), sigma_x ~ 0.9 mm residual (tracker-limited) — with the co-registration chain (module center (6.58, 4.66) mm, MCP-RADiCAL alignment 0.79 mm). Frame explicitly as the per-cell measurement set needed for muon-collider BIB rejection and FCC-hh pile-up assignment: time window + position + energy from one compact device, simultaneously.

NECESSARY (the 5D figure — cheap, data-ready): the depth-scale calibration <(DW-UP)/2> vs ln(E) over the DSB1 25-150 GeV scan. Prediction: shower max deepens ~1 X0 per e-fold of energy, and X0/v_fiber = 5.4 mm / 200 mm/ns = 27 ps/X0, so the mean should shift ~48 ps from 25 to 150 GeV — trivially resolvable with 10^4-10^5 events per energy (mean known to <1 ps). A measured slope consistent with 27 ps/X0 converts the dual-end timing from 'floor' into a calibrated longitudinal coordinate with per-event sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-6 mm ~ 1 X0.

NECESSARY (truth-reference section): one subsection on the wire chamber itself — the delay-line method, kWC_Scale = 7/36 mm/ns, the self-calibrated sigma ~ 3.6 mm (digitizer-limited at 1 GS/s, not wire pitch), and the ~2.9 mm beam-spot spread — so readers can weigh every position claim; this is what makes the comparator-limited framing credible rather than evasive.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: An honest, defensible claim: the FIRST single-module, same-event beam-test demonstration of 4D (x, y, E, t) calorimetry AT SHOWER MAX — one 14x14x135 mm^3 device simultaneously measuring time (35-57 ps at 150 GeV, clock-free (DW-UP)/2; 25.3 ps on the DSB1 build with the lgcfd method), shower-max energy (sigma_E/E ~ 12-14%, WLS-species-independent), and transverse position (residual <= 1.5 mm against a ~3.6 mm tracker) on the same TENERGY event sample, with a co-registered frame (0.79 mm MCP-module alignment). Additionally claimable: dual-end readout makes the module depth-SENSITIVE in principle (the timing floor itself is the ~1 X0 depth fluctuation, sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-6 mm).
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Cannot claim: (1) 5D as a demonstrated per-event coordinate — no figure yet shows <(DW-UP)/2> tracking known depth variation; (2) calibrated ~1 mm position (tracker-comparator-limited at 3.6 mm); (3) module-level 10%/sqrtE energy (all energy numbers are shower-max sampling with a ~9% constant term); (4) 25 ps ABSOLUTE time-of-flight — the 25-27 ps is the self-referenced differential, while the absolute shower time vs MCP sits near ~50 ps until the depth-corrected estimator lands; (5) 'imaging' or photon-pointing — one longitudinal sample, electrons not photons, one energy point (150 GeV) for the 4D demo, one module (no array).
fourD_verdict.unlock: Two minimal analyses upgrade the claim. (1) For honest 5D: the <(DW-UP)/2>-vs-ln(E) depth calibration on existing DSB1 reduced data — verify the ~27 ps/X0 slope (predicted ~48 ps mean shift over 25->150 GeV) and quote per-event sigma_z = v_fiber*sigma_t ~ 4-6 mm ~ 1 X0; that converts the floor into a measured fifth coordinate at shower max ('5D = x, y, z_depth, E, t'). (2) For the stronger timing claim: finish the depth-corrected absolute shower-time estimator (requires the reducer-level absolute-timebase fix + re-reduction) so an absolute ToF number, not just the differential, can be quoted against collider use cases. Extending the 4D demo from 150 GeV to all five TENERGY energies is nearly free and removes the one-energy-point objection.

killer_additions: The event-mixing two-shower separation study: overlay single-electron events at wire-chamber-known offsets and publish two-shower identification efficiency + centroid pull vs separation and energy, then map it through E_max ~ 2*m_pi0*L/s_min into 'pi0/gamma separable up to E ~ X GeV at lever arm L' for collider and forward geometries. No new data required, and it is the figure phenomenologists would actually cite — it turns a position residual into a physics-reach curve.

A one-page 'physics reach' table converting each build's measured (a, b) into benchmarks: c*sigma_t vertex discrimination (25 ps -> 7.6 mm vs ~45-50 mm luminous region for H->gamma gamma), FCC-hh pile-up time-slicing factor (~x7 vs ~170 ps collision spread, PU~1000), muon-collider BIB window (+/-50 ps at 2sigma), and the rad-hard punchline that ceramic LuAG:Ce costs only light (a: 201->455) and not the floor (b ~ 20 ps shared, in-event ratio 0.99) — making RADiCAL the quantitative reference point for compact rad-hard 4D EM cells.

The 'floor is the measurement' inversion as a named result: the depth-calibration figure (<(DW-UP)/2> vs ln E, measured slope vs the 27 ps/X0 = X0/v_fiber prediction) reframes the apparent 20 ps limitation as shower-depth metrology with sigma_z ~ 1 X0 — a memorable conceptual claim that no competing fast-timing calorimeter (MTD, Crilin, HGCAL cells) can make from a single longitudinal sample, and the legitimate basis of the 5D positioning.
referee_risks: Conflating the differential (DW-UP)/2 with absolute time-of-arrival in any physics-reach statement: H->gamma gamma vertexing, delayed-photon, and BIB-window arguments need the absolute clock-referenced time (~50 ps here with led), not the 25 ps self-referenced depth observable. Quote both, label both, map each physics case to the right one.

Headlining 0.9 or 1.5 mm 'position resolution' when the truth tracker measures sigma ~ 3.6 mm — must be presented as a comparator-limited upper bound, with the wire-chamber resolution measurement shown; likewise any position-dependent analysis binned finer than ~3.5-4 mm is smearing-correlated.

Letting shower-max sigma_E/E ~ 12-14% (or the 9.31% constant term) be read as the module/technology energy resolution against the 10%/sqrtE + 0.7% program goal — every energy number needs the 'restricted shower-max sampling, ~3 tiles, ~1 R_M' qualifier plus the full-module/3x3 projection.

Presenting the extrapolated floor b ~ 19.5 ps as achieved: LuAG's brightest measured point is still ~42 ps and no beam exists above 150 GeV; headline the MEASURED sigma@150 and quote b as a form-dependent (1/sqrtE vs 1/E) extrapolation with the per-technique caveat (cfd05 vs lgcfd floors are 4.2-sigma distinct).

Any pi0-rejection or photon-physics claim from electron-only data without flagging it: single electrons at normal incidence, no photon conversions, no measured two-particle events — event mixing must be labeled a pattern-recognition demonstration, not a measured pi0 rejection; e- vs gamma shower-start differences are unstudied.

The MIXED kill-shot (ratio 0.99) rests on a DATA-INFERRED corner material map flagged CONFIRM-against-logbook (NW match weakest) — confirm before publication or a referee can void the paper's central confound-free argument; same for the unconfirmed LuAG-build NW capillary type.

Collider-reach numbers (FCC-hh PU slicing, muon-collider BIB) presented without the 'single module, normal incidence, electrons, extrapolation' disclaimer; the 4D demo currently rests on one energy point (150 GeV) in one build, and beam momentum spread (~1% literature, not measured) should be propagated into the energy-resolution constant term discussion.

fourD_verdict.can_claim: An honest 4D (x,y,E,t) demonstration in a single module IS supportable from TENERGY 150 GeV: per-event energy (sigma_E/E ~12-14% shower-max, LG chain, DRS1), transverse position (~0.9-1.5 mm residual, tracker-comparator-limited), and time -- all from one co-registered DAQ event. From the digitizer lens the claim is clean BECAUSE each observable is internally referenced: (DW-UP)/2 lives in one DRS4 group (domino phase cancels exactly), energy is a same-board LG sum, position is a same-board delay-line differential. No inter-board sync is required for the 4D vector, and TR0a/TR0b in every group provides the event-integrity cross-check. A SOFT 5D statement is also supportable: (DW-UP)/2 is a per-event longitudinal-depth observable (v_fiber ~200 mm/ns), and its measured spread matches the ~1 X0 shower-max fluctuation -- 'per-event sensitivity to shower depth' is defensible today.
fourD_verdict.cannot_claim: Three things. (1) The 't' in 4D as currently drawn (fourD_demo.png Panel 1) is (DW-UP)/2 -- a SELF-difference whose spread is depth fluctuation, not a timestamp against an external clock. A referee will say 27-57 ps 'time' here is not a time-of-arrival resolution. The true absolute time T=mean(hg_led) vs MCP floors near ~50 ps and, cross-group, inherits the 71-74 ps domino phase unless stop-cell-corrected. (2) A quantified 5D 'depth measured to X mm per event' -- there is no independent depth truth (no longitudinal segmentation), so only sensitivity, not calibrated resolution, is currently demonstrable. (3) Any 10 ps / full-module-energy / sub-mm program-goal language: 14% constant term is shower-max sampling only, 1.5 mm sits below the 3.6 mm WC comparator, and the timing floor is 17.5-20 ps.
fourD_verdict.unlock: Two cheap analyses, both already in the reduced trees. (1) The 5D unlock: plot the MEAN of (DW-UP)*v_fiber/2 vs beam energy and overlay the GEANT4 shower-max depth (paper Fig 7 shows max moving from layers 8-10 at low E to 11-13 at 150 GeV, ~ln(E)); if the centroid tracks the predicted few-mm shift, depth is MEASURED, not inferred. Corroborate within one energy by correlating per-event (DW-UP)/2 with the Pb-glass leakage fraction sum_pb (deeper showers leak more). That converts the floor story into a fifth coordinate. (2) The honest-t unlock: quote the 4D time axis as stop-cell-corrected mean(hg_led) referenced to the same-group MCP copy (the mcp_jitter_canon machinery already shows 71->~15 ps after correction); report that sigma_t_abs alongside the (DW-UP)/2 depth observable instead of conflating them. With both, '5D (x,y,z_depth,E,t) shower metrology in one 14x14x135 mm module' is defensible in NIM A.

killer_additions: A single 'digitizer forensics' control figure (3-4 panels, one page) that measures EVERY DRS4 systematic in-data: (a) sigma(MCP1-MCP2)/sqrt2 vs E -- flat 71-74 ps, amplitude-independent, dropping to ~15 ps after stop-cell correction (proves it is domino phase, not the MCP, and that same-group differentials cancel it exactly); (b) sigma_t vs stop-cell index -- flat curve proves no residual fixed-pattern timing INL because the uniformly rotating stop cell sweeps the beam crossing over all 1024 physical cells; (c) the 0.2 ns satellite present in cfd05 and absent in lgcfd on the SAME events. This flips the switched-capacitor digitizer from referee liability into a demonstrated-control showcase, and becomes the citable calibration reference for every future RADiCAL campaign.

The 5D upgrade: mean[(DW-UP)/2]*v_fiber vs E overlaid on the GEANT4 ln(E) shower-max depth curve, plus the in-event (DW-UP)/2 vs Pb-glass-leakage correlation at fixed energy. First per-event longitudinal shower-depth measurement from dual-end WLS timing in a shashlik -- it converts 'the floor is depth physics' (an excuse) into 'we measure depth' (a coordinate), and it is the single most memorable claim available in this dataset.

A transferable picosecond budget table answering 'is 5 GS/s the limit?': aperture/cell-width <1 ps, interpolation-on-the-recovered-edge ~1-3 ps (quantified vs crossing phase), slew 2-7 ps, inter-group reference 71-74 ps (cancels in-group / ~15 ps corrected), shower-depth physics 17-20 ps.
