Conclusion the community can reuse: 10 GS/s or firmware CFD buys NOTHING on the (DW-UP)/2 floor (depth-physics-limited) but directly attacks the absolute-time channel (finer interpolation on the 381 mV/ns recovered edge, satellite-free thresholds, and -- with a distributed clock -- removal of the inter-chip phase). That paragraph is the honest answer to 'why not a better digitizer' and the design requirement for the 18x18 module DAQ.
experts.lens: Waveform digitization expert — DRS4/CAEN DT5742 switched-capacitor systematics: fixed-pattern timing INL, stop-cell/domino phase, inter-chip sync, deliberate-saturation dual-gain strategy, sampling-rate limits vs the timing floor
paper1_assessment: [+] The deliberate-saturation dual-gain strategy is clearly stated and physically sound (Sec 4 + Fig 12): HG 'driven further into saturation to maximize the signal rise', LG kept linear for energy. Documenting design intent is what makes the follow-up edge-recovery (lgcfd) publishable as a method rather than a patch.

[+] Rare honesty about a digitizer-level artifact: the ~0.2 ns satellite (Fig 19, ~15% of events) is correctly diagnosed as a one-sample (0.2 ns at 5 GS/s) shift of the fixed-threshold crossing, and correctly noted to be absent in the MCP-independent (DW-UP)/2. Most beam-test papers would have hidden this.

[+] The BestMinus/(DW-UP)/2 adoption (Fig 27) implicitly exploits same-group DRS4 differential cancellation -- the 17.5 ps floor sits far below the (unstated) 71-74 ps inter-chip phase jitter, which is only possible because corners NW/NE/SE share one group with their MCP copy. The result is right even though the mechanism is never explained.

[-] No digitizer calibration section at all: sampling rate appears once as '5GS/s and 12 bit' (Sec 4), the CAEN reference [23] is a product URL, and there is no statement of cell-width (timing INL) calibration, stop-cell handling, or per-cell pedestal treatment. For a 17.5 ps floor claimed from a switched-capacitor ASIC, that omission is the single most attackable gap; the data (cell-width RMS <1 ps on DRS0, stop-cell uniform over [0,1023]) would have answered it in one paragraph.

[-] Physics misattribution in Sec 5.2.1/Fig 19: the broader (DW+UP)/2 is attributed 'primarily to the presence and contribution of the MCP resolution', and BestPlus-MCP subtracts sigma~10 ps for the MCP. The measured sigma(MCP1-MCP2)/sqrt2 = 71-74 ps -- flat with energy, amplitude-independent -- shows the dominant term is inter-group DRS4 domino/stop-cell phase, NOT the ~10 ps MCP (which is common-mode in the split signal and cancels). The published MCP-referenced methods (Fig 27 Left, 49-95 ps) are partly digitizer-sync-limited and the paper does not say so.

[-] The clipping regime is never quantified: the effective clip is ~820 mV (DC-offset-lowered DT5742 channel saturation, not the nominal ~950 mV and not SiPM pixel saturation), and 71-94% of pulses are clipped above 50 GeV. So 'cfd05' is in reality a ~41 mV fixed threshold on the foot of a saturated pulse at high energy -- the reader cannot assess time-walk vs energy without the clipped-fraction table.

[-] No in-situ demonstration of the MCP reference performance: '10 < sigma_t < 20 ps depending on detection region' is asserted (Sec 4) but never measured in the data, and the single-tube passive split into both DT5742 groups -- the design choice that makes the whole reference scheme work -- is not described.
paper2_timing: A dedicated 'Digitizer timebase and synchronization' subsection (one column + one table): both board rates (DRS0 5 GS/s / 0.2 ns per cell for all timing channels incl. both MCP copies; DRS1 1 GS/s for LG+WC); stop-cell recovery (single zero-width step, stopcell[4], uniform RMS 296-298 = 1024/sqrt12 confirming asynchronous trigger); measured DRS0 cell-width RMS <1 ps (worded as 'bounded by the float32 storage precision', not literally 0.84 ps); per-group MCP referencing of every stored crossing. Cite the DRS4 design paper (S. Ritt et al.) and a DRS4 local-timebase-calibration reference (e.g. Stricker-Shaver/Ritt/Pichler, IEEE TNS 61 (2014)) plus the DT5742 manual -- state explicitly which calibrations are applied and which are demonstrably unnecessary for same-group differentials.

The mcp_jitter_canon figure promoted to a numbered paper figure: sigma(MCP1-MCP2)/sqrt2 vs E, flat at 71-74 ps and amplitude-independent (= clock, not slew); ~15 ps after OOS stop-cell correction. One figure simultaneously (a) measures the inter-chip sync, (b) proves the MCP intrinsic ~10 ps is common-mode in the split signal, (c) justifies why (DW-UP)/2 in one group sits below the per-group reference floor, and (d) gently corrects the parent paper's 'MCP resolution' attribution without contradicting any published number.

Satellite-kill demonstration (checkable claim -- demonstrate, never assert): same-event cfd05 vs lgcfd (DW-UP)/2 and per-channel delta-t distributions showing the 0.2 ns one-sample satellite population present/absent; quantify the residual satellite fraction and the Delta-sigma; cite parent Fig 18/19 explicitly as the documented artifact this method removes.

Clipping-recovery validity package: (i) clipped-fraction vs E table (4/71/90/93-94% at 25-150 GeV) with the statement that the 820 mV clip is DT5742 channel saturation under the deliberate DC offset, not SiPM pixel saturation (evidence: HG rising-edge dV/dt keeps rising 102->279 mV/ns with energy); (ii) the hglg calibration figure (HG=a+b*LG hockey-stick, fit on UNCLIPPED low-E events only, slope drift <=2% across energies => one calibration per channel is valid); (iii) closure test in the regime where truth exists -- on unclipped events (25 GeV / dim builds) lgcfd and cfd05 must agree within errors. Without (iii) the extrapolation from unclipped calibration to 94%-clipped data is the referee's first attack.

Sampling-phase/interpolation systematic, quantified: the recovered edge (~381 mV/ns) traverses the 15% threshold-to-clip span in only a few 0.2 ns samples; show the linear-interpolation timing bias vs crossing phase within a cell (toy or in-data via crossing-cell binning) and that it is small vs the floor. Companion fixed-pattern check: because the stop cell rotates uniformly while the beam sits at a fixed window position (~91.3 ns, cell ~456), the crossing samples all 1024 physical cells -- plot sigma_t (and mean t) vs stop-cell index; flatness IS the in-data proof that timing INL does not bias the result.

The 'is 5 GS/s the limit?' budget paragraph: aperture <1 ps + interpolation ~1-3 ps + slew 2-7 ps (recovered edge) + reference 0 (same-group) vs the 17.5-20 ps depth-physics floor => sampling rate is NOT the limiting factor for the differential headline; 10 GS/s or on-board constant-fraction firmware would buy improvement only on the absolute-time channel and at the lowest light levels (LuAG 25-50 GeV, where cfd05's 5% foot sits in noise). This preempts the 'use a better digitizer' review comment and feeds the 18x18-module DAQ design.

Method-survey hygiene for the 8-source comparison (cfd03..cfd50, led, lgcfd across DSB1/MIXED/LuAG/TENERGY): state per build WHY the winner wins in digitizer terms -- lgcfd for clipped/bright (threshold on the recovered steep edge, satellite-free), led for dim/unclipped (fixed 20 mV = 4x the 1.31 mV pedestal RMS; immune to peak mismeasurement) -- and keep the source choice OUT of the systematics table (it is the thesis, per the locked plan). Include the sigma-estimator audit (robust fit window + channel-consistency veto) as one appendix paragraph so the monotonicity fix is on record.
paper3_energy_position: State the wire-chamber comparator is DIGITIZER-limited, then exploit it: the delay-line WC is read on DRS1 at 1 GS/s, so sigma_x ~3.6 mm comes from 1 ns sampling on a 7/36 mm/ns delay line (~0.194 mm per ns; ~0.25 mm quantization steps), not wire pitch; the sub-sample CFD-50 variant (~3.3 mm) shows the ceiling. Mandatory wording: the 1.5 mm (and 0.9 mm TENERGY) capillary residual is COMPARATOR-LIMITED -- 'capillary and tracker each <=1.5 mm', never 'capillary resolution = 1.5 mm'. Enforce the >=3.5-4 mm binning guardrail in every position-dependent plot.

LG energy-chain digitization control: pedestal method (samples 3-52), the AC-coupling ~35% undershoot (150-180 ns) and proof the charge window [imin-15, imin+200] excludes it; LG operating range far from the DRS1 rails at 150 GeV (no compression -- the 9-20% droop is shower leakage, Pb-glass-corrected 9.2%->2.2%, not ADC nonlinearity); one table line showing 12-bit LSB (~0.25 mV) and 1.31 mV pedestal RMS contribute negligibly to the 9.31% constant term. Note DRS1 cell-width (~4.8 ps RMS) is irrelevant for amplitude/area observables.

E-type characterization with an explicit noise floor: at 1.6 mV/GeV the E-type NW channel delivers ~80 mV at 50 GeV -- quantify S/N vs energy and show its constant-term-dominated, nearly flat sigma_E/E is light-collection physics, not digitization/noise. Pair with the in-beam analog of published Fig 4 (E-type uniform vs T-type localized).

4D co-registration statement from the DAQ side: time (DRS0), energy (DRS1 LG), position (DRS1 WC) come from two free-running, non-phase-locked boards -- the 4D claim survives because each axis is internally referenced within one board/group; TR0 digitized in all four groups (t_TR0a - t_TR0b) demonstrates event-alignment integrity. One paragraph plus one TR0-sync cross-check plot kills the 'how do you know the boards saw the same event' question.

Fix the 4D time axis before the capstone figure ships: replace (or supplement) the (DW-UP)/2 panel in fourD_demo.png with the stop-cell-corrected absolute shower time mean(hg_led) vs the same-group MCP copy -- (DW-UP)/2 is the DEPTH observable in this paper's own bridge narrative, so using it as 'time' in the 4D panel invites the self-difference objection. The depth_corr/absShowerTime machinery already exists; the reducer-level absolute-timebase fix it needs should be scheduled as a Paper-3 prerequisite.

Energy linearity figure tying back to the published 29.4 mV/GeV with the multi-build LG slopes (~55 mV/GeV chain quoted in the outline -- reconcile the two normalizations explicitly in the text so the parent paper and this one cannot be played against each other), plus the per-channel HG-LG correlation (rho 0.73-0.95 over all 32 channels) as the cross-board consistency check that also certifies MIXED LG health.
referee_risks: 'A 17.5-20 ps floor from a switched-capacitor digitizer with no published timebase calibration' -- the parent paper has nothing; Paper 2 MUST show cell-width (<1 ps), stop-cell statistics, and the sigma_t-vs-stop-cell flatness, or the floor claim is soft.

lgcfd extrapolation validity: calibrated on unclipped low-E, applied to 94%-clipped 150 GeV data. Defenses needed in-paper: slope energy-independence (<=2% drift), the ~5% fit-range nonlinearity acknowledged, rising dV/dt 102->279 mV/ns (no SiPM saturation), and the unclipped-regime closure test vs cfd05.

The published-text attribution of (DW+UP)/2 broadening to 'MCP resolution' vs the measured 71-74 ps inter-group domino phase -- Paper 2 must correct this WITHOUT appearing to contradict the parent paper (frame as 'we now separate the reference contributions'; the published headline used (DW-UP)/2, which is immune, so no published number changes).

Satellite removal claimed but not demonstrated -- it is a checkable one-sample artifact; assert-only gets a major-revision comment. Same for 'the floor is shower-depth physics' if the mean-(DW-UP)-vs-E depth signature is not shown.
Position: quoting 1.5 mm (or 0.9 mm) as a resolution when the WC comparator is 3.6 mm -- guaranteed objection; comparator-limited wording is non-negotiable.

4D 't' axis built from (DW-UP)/2: a self-referenced difference is not a timestamp resolution against an external clock; quote the absolute mean(hg_led) number (with its higher floor) alongside, or the 4D claim gets reduced in review.

Deliberate saturation of 71-94% of timing pulses: expect 'you timed on saturated signals' -- needs the clipped-fraction table, the foot-timing/time-walk explanation of why cfd05 degraded, and the recovered-edge mechanism stated as the design-intended dual-gain completion.

Floor-form honesty: b is a per-technique extrapolation beyond 150 GeV (cfd05 vs lgcfd floors are 4.2-sigma distinct); forcing one shared floor, or headlining the extrapolated b instead of the measured 25.3 ps at 150 GeV, will be caught. LuAG's brightest 150 GeV point never reaches ~20 ps in data -- say so plainly.

Soft spots to close before submission: MIXED corner-material map is data-inferred (confirm against the logbook -- the in-event kill-shot depends on which corners are which), and the LuAG-build NW capillary type is unconfirmed; either would be embarrassing if a collaborator catches it post-submission.
